title.zh: 卷二十五下 郊祀志
title.raw: 卷二十五下 郊祀志 Volume 25b: Treatise on Sacrifices 2

sentences.zh: 是時既滅兩粵，粵人勇之乃言「粵人俗鬼，而其祠皆見鬼，數有效。

translations.literal: Literal: After the two Yue states were destroyed, a Yue man named Yongzhi said: "Yue customs revere spirits, and in their rites spirits appear, often with real efficacy.
translations.idiomatic: After the two Yue states were destroyed, a Yue man named Yongzhi said: "Yue customs revere spirits, and in their rites spirits appear, often with real efficacy.

sentences.zh: 昔東甌王敬鬼，壽百六十歲。

translations.literal: Literal: In former times the King of Eastern Ou revered spirits and lived to one hundred sixty.
translations.idiomatic: In former times the King of Eastern Ou revered spirits and lived to one hundred sixty.

sentences.zh: 後世怠嫚，故衰耗。」

translations.literal: Literal: Later generations grew lax and irreverent, and so declined and weakened."
translations.idiomatic: Later generations grew lax and irreverent, and so declined and weakened."

sentences.zh: 乃命粵巫立粵祝祠，安臺無壇，亦祠天神帝百鬼，而以雞卜。

translations.literal: Literal: So he ordered Yue shamans to establish Yue invocatory rites: no raised altars, but sacrifices to heavenly gods, the Thearch, and the hundred spirits, using chicken divination.
translations.idiomatic: So he ordered Yue shamans to establish Yue invocatory rites: no raised altars, but sacrifices to heavenly gods, the Thearch, and the hundred spirits, using chicken divination.

sentences.zh: 上信之，粵祠雞卜自此始用。

translations.literal: Literal: The emperor believed this, and from then on chicken divination was used in Yue rites.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor believed this, and from then on chicken divination was used in Yue rites.

sentences.zh: 公孫卿曰：「僊人可見，上往常遽，以故不見。

translations.literal: Literal: Gongsun Qing said: "Transcendents can be seen, but Your Majesty has always gone in haste, and so has not seen them.
translations.idiomatic: Gongsun Qing said: "Transcendents can be seen, but Your Majesty has always gone in haste, and so has not seen them.

sentences.zh: 今陛下可為館如緱氏城，置脯棗，神人宜可致。

translations.literal: Literal: If Your Majesty builds lodgings like Goushi City and sets out dried meat and dates, spirit-men should be summonable.
translations.idiomatic: If Your Majesty builds lodgings like Goushi City and sets out dried meat and dates, spirit-men should be summonable.

sentences.zh: 且僊人好樓居。」

translations.literal: Literal: And transcendents prefer to dwell in towers."
translations.idiomatic: And transcendents prefer to dwell in towers."

sentences.zh: 於是上令長安則作飛廉、桂館，甘泉則作益壽、延壽館，使卿持節設具而候神人。

translations.literal: Literal: So the emperor ordered Feilian and Gui pavilions built at Chang'an, and Yishou and Yanshou pavilions at Ganquan, with Qing bearing tally and preparing offerings to await spirit-men.
translations.idiomatic: So the emperor ordered Feilian and Gui pavilions built at Chang'an, and Yishou and Yanshou pavilions at Ganquan, with Qing bearing tally and preparing offerings to await spirit-men.

sentences.zh: 乃作通天臺，置祠具其下，將招來神僊之屬。

translations.literal: Literal: He then built the Tongtian Terrace, with ritual implements beneath it, intending to summon divine immortals.
translations.idiomatic: He then built the Tongtian Terrace, with ritual implements beneath it, intending to summon divine immortals.

sentences.zh: 於是甘泉更置前殿，始廣諸宮室。

translations.literal: Literal: Accordingly, a new front hall was added at Ganquan, beginning a broad expansion of palace buildings.
translations.idiomatic: Accordingly, a new front hall was added at Ganquan, beginning a broad expansion of palace buildings.

sentences.zh: 夏，有芝生甘泉殿房內中。

translations.literal: Literal: That summer, zhi fungus grew inside the chamber rooms of Ganquan Hall.
translations.idiomatic: That summer, zhi fungus grew inside the chamber rooms of Ganquan Hall.

sentences.zh: 天子為塞河，興通天，若有光云，乃下詔赦天下。

translations.literal: Literal: While the Son of Heaven was blocking the river and building Tongtian, there was said to be strange light, and he issued an amnesty for the realm.
translations.idiomatic: While the Son of Heaven was blocking the river and building Tongtian, there was said to be strange light, and he issued an amnesty for the realm.

sentences.zh: 其明年，伐朝鮮。

translations.literal: Literal: The following year, he campaigned against Joseon.
translations.idiomatic: The following year, he campaigned against Joseon.

sentences.zh: 夏，旱。

translations.literal: Literal: In summer there was drought.
translations.idiomatic: In summer there was drought.

sentences.zh: 公孫卿曰：「黃帝時封則天旱，乾封三年。」

translations.literal: Literal: Gongsun Qing said: "In the Yellow Thearch's time, when Feng rites were performed, there was also drought; Qianfeng lasted three years."
translations.idiomatic: Gongsun Qing said: "In the Yellow Thearch's time, when Feng rites were performed, there was also drought; Qianfeng lasted three years."

sentences.zh: 上乃下詔：「天旱，意乾封乎？

translations.literal: Literal: So the emperor issued an edict: "This drought, could it mean Qianfeng?
translations.idiomatic: So the emperor issued an edict: "This drought, could it mean Qianfeng?

sentences.zh: 其令天下尊祠靈星焉。」

translations.literal: Literal: Order the realm to honor and sacrifice to the Spirit Star."
translations.idiomatic: Order the realm to honor and sacrifice to the Spirit Star."

sentences.zh: 明年，上郊雍五畤，通回中道，遂北出蕭關，歷獨鹿、鳴澤，自西河歸，幸河東祠后土。

translations.literal: Literal: The next year the emperor held suburban rites at Yong's five altars, opened the Huizhong route, then went north out Xiao Pass, passed Dulu and Ming Marsh, returned by Xihe, and visited Hedong to sacrifice to Houtu.
translations.idiomatic: The next year the emperor held suburban rites at Yong's five altars, opened the Huizhong route, then went north out Xiao Pass, passed Dulu and Ming Marsh, returned by Xihe, and visited Hedong to sacrifice to Houtu.

sentences.zh: 明年冬，上巡南郡，至江陵而東。

translations.literal: Literal: The following winter he inspected Nanjun, reached Jiangling, and continued east.
translations.idiomatic: The following winter he inspected Nanjun, reached Jiangling, and continued east.

sentences.zh: 登禮灊之天柱山，號曰南嶽。

translations.literal: Literal: He ascended and ritually honored Tianzhu Mountain at Qian, naming it the Southern Peak.
translations.idiomatic: He ascended and ritually honored Tianzhu Mountain at Qian, naming it the Southern Peak.

sentences.zh: 浮江，自潯陽出樅陽，過彭蠡，禮其名山川。

translations.literal: Literal: He floated down the river, from Xunyang out by Zongyang, passed Pengli, and performed rites at famous mountains and rivers.
translations.idiomatic: He floated down the river, from Xunyang out by Zongyang, passed Pengli, and performed rites at famous mountains and rivers.

sentences.zh: 北至琅邪，並海上。

translations.literal: Literal: He went north to Langya, traveling along the coast.
translations.idiomatic: He went north to Langya, traveling along the coast.

sentences.zh: 四月，至奉高修封焉。

translations.literal: Literal: In the fourth month he reached Fenggao and renewed the Feng rites.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth month he reached Fenggao and renewed the Feng rites.

sentences.zh: 初，天子封泰山，泰山東北阯古時有明堂處，處險不敞。

translations.literal: Literal: Originally, when the Son of Heaven sealed Mount Tai, there was an ancient Mingtang site northeast below the mountain, but it was steep and cramped.
translations.idiomatic: Originally, when the Son of Heaven sealed Mount Tai, there was an ancient Mingtang site northeast below the mountain, but it was steep and cramped.

sentences.zh: 上欲治明堂奉高旁，未曉其制度。

translations.literal: Literal: The emperor wanted to build a Mingtang near Fenggao but did not understand its design.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor wanted to build a Mingtang near Fenggao but did not understand its design.

sentences.zh: 濟南人公玉帶上黃帝時明堂圖。

translations.literal: Literal: A man of Jinan, Gongyu Dai, presented a Mingtang diagram from the Yellow Thearch's time.
translations.idiomatic: A man of Jinan, Gongyu Dai, presented a Mingtang diagram from the Yellow Thearch's time.

sentences.zh: 明堂中有一殿，四面無壁，以茅蓋，通水，水圜宮垣，為復道，上有樓，從西南入，名曰昆侖，天子從之入，以拜祀上帝焉。

translations.literal: Literal: In it, the Mingtang had one central hall, open on all four sides with no walls, thatched roof, running water, and a circular moat around the palace wall, with an elevated passage and tower above; entry was from the southwest, called Kunlun, through which the Son of Heaven entered to bow and sacrifice to the High Thearch.
translations.idiomatic: In it, the Mingtang had one central hall, open on all four sides with no walls, thatched roof, running water, and a circular moat around the palace wall, with an elevated passage and tower above; entry was from the southwest, called Kunlun, through which the Son of Heaven entered to bow and sacrifice to the High Thearch.

sentences.zh: 於是上令奉高作明堂汶上，如帶圖。

translations.literal: Literal: So the emperor ordered a Mingtang built above the Wen River at Fenggao according to Dai's diagram.
translations.idiomatic: So the emperor ordered a Mingtang built above the Wen River at Fenggao according to Dai's diagram.

sentences.zh: 及是歲修封，則祠泰一、五帝於明堂上坐，合高皇帝祠坐對之。

translations.literal: Literal: That year, when renewing Feng rites, he sacrificed to Taiyi and the Five Thearchs at the upper seats of the Mingtang, with the seat for Gao Emperor's shrine facing them.
translations.idiomatic: That year, when renewing Feng rites, he sacrificed to Taiyi and the Five Thearchs at the upper seats of the Mingtang, with the seat for Gao Emperor's shrine facing them.

sentences.zh: 祠后土於下房，以二十太牢。

translations.literal: Literal: He sacrificed to Houtu in the lower chamber, using twenty full ox victims.
translations.idiomatic: He sacrificed to Houtu in the lower chamber, using twenty full ox victims.

sentences.zh: 天子從昆侖道入，始拜明堂如郊禮。

translations.literal: Literal: The Son of Heaven entered by the Kunlun way and for the first time bowed in the Mingtang according to suburban rites.
translations.idiomatic: The Son of Heaven entered by the Kunlun way and for the first time bowed in the Mingtang according to suburban rites.

sentences.zh: 畢，抠堂下。

translations.literal: Literal: After completion, he withdrew below the hall.
translations.idiomatic: After completion, he withdrew below the hall.

sentences.zh: 而上又上泰山，自有祕祠其顛。

translations.literal: Literal: Then the emperor ascended Mount Tai again and held secret rites at the summit.
translations.idiomatic: Then the emperor ascended Mount Tai again and held secret rites at the summit.

sentences.zh: 而泰山下祠五帝，各如其方，黃帝并赤帝所，有司侍祠焉。

translations.literal: Literal: Below Mount Tai, sacrifices to the Five Thearchs were arranged by direction; the Yellow Thearch was combined with the Red Thearch site, and officials attended the rites.
translations.idiomatic: Below Mount Tai, sacrifices to the Five Thearchs were arranged by direction; the Yellow Thearch was combined with the Red Thearch site, and officials attended the rites.

sentences.zh: 山上舉火，下悉應之。

translations.literal: Literal: Fires were raised on the mountain, and all below answered with corresponding fires.
translations.idiomatic: Fires were raised on the mountain, and all below answered with corresponding fires.

sentences.zh: 還幸甘泉，郊泰畤。

translations.literal: Literal: He returned to Ganquan and held suburban rites at the Tai altar.
translations.idiomatic: He returned to Ganquan and held suburban rites at the Tai altar.

sentences.zh: 春幸汾陰，祠后土。

translations.literal: Literal: In spring he visited Fenyin and sacrificed to Houtu.
translations.idiomatic: In spring he visited Fenyin and sacrificed to Houtu.

sentences.zh: 明年，幸泰山，以十一月甲子朔旦冬至日祀上帝於明堂，後每修封。

translations.literal: Literal: The next year he visited Mount Tai and on the jiazi new-moon dawn at winter solstice in the eleventh month sacrificed to the High Thearch in the Mingtang; afterward this was done each time Feng rites were renewed.
translations.idiomatic: The next year he visited Mount Tai and on the jiazi new-moon dawn at winter solstice in the eleventh month sacrificed to the High Thearch in the Mingtang; afterward this was done each time Feng rites were renewed.

sentences.zh: 其贊饗曰：「天增授皇帝泰元神策，周而復始。

translations.literal: Literal: The offering hymn said: "Heaven has additionally bestowed on the emperor the great-origin divine register, cycling and beginning again.
translations.idiomatic: The offering hymn said: "Heaven has additionally bestowed on the emperor the great-origin divine register, cycling and beginning again.

sentences.zh: 皇帝敬拜泰一。」

translations.literal: Literal: The emperor reverently bows to Taiyi."
translations.idiomatic: The emperor reverently bows to Taiyi."

sentences.zh: 東至海上，考入海及方士求神者，莫驗，然益遣，幾遇之。

translations.literal: Literal: Going east to the coast, he investigated sea expeditions and spirit-seekers among the fangshi; nothing could be verified, yet he sent out more, hoping to encounter them.
translations.idiomatic: Going east to the coast, he investigated sea expeditions and spirit-seekers among the fangshi; nothing could be verified, yet he sent out more, hoping to encounter them.

sentences.zh: 乙酉，柏梁災。

translations.literal: Literal: On yiyou day, Boliang burned.
translations.idiomatic: On yiyou day, Boliang burned.

sentences.zh: 十二月甲午朔，上親禪高里，祠后土。

translations.literal: Literal: On jiawu new moon of the twelfth month, the emperor personally performed Shan rite at Gaoli and sacrificed to Houtu.
translations.idiomatic: On jiawu new moon of the twelfth month, the emperor personally performed Shan rite at Gaoli and sacrificed to Houtu.

sentences.zh: 臨勃海，將以望祀蓬萊之屬，幾至殊庭焉。

translations.literal: Literal: He came to the Bohai shore, intending distant sacrifice to Penglai and the like, nearly reaching the strange court.
translations.idiomatic: He came to the Bohai shore, intending distant sacrifice to Penglai and the like, nearly reaching the strange court.

sentences.zh: 上還，以柏梁災故，受計甘泉。

translations.literal: Literal: On returning, because of the Boliang fire, he held assessments at Ganquan.
translations.idiomatic: On returning, because of the Boliang fire, he held assessments at Ganquan.

sentences.zh: 公孫卿曰：「黃帝就青靈臺，十二日燒，黃帝乃治明庭。

translations.literal: Literal: Gongsun Qing said: "The Yellow Thearch went to the Qingling Terrace, which burned for twelve days, and only then did the Yellow Thearch build Mingtang.
translations.idiomatic: Gongsun Qing said: "The Yellow Thearch went to the Qingling Terrace, which burned for twelve days, and only then did the Yellow Thearch build Mingtang.

sentences.zh: 明庭，甘泉也。」

translations.literal: Literal: Mingtang means Ganquan."
translations.idiomatic: Mingtang means Ganquan."

sentences.zh: 方士多言古帝王有都甘泉者。

translations.literal: Literal: Many fangshi said ancient emperors had capitals at Ganquan.
translations.idiomatic: Many fangshi said ancient emperors had capitals at Ganquan.

sentences.zh: 其後天子又朝諸侯甘泉，甘泉作諸侯邸。

translations.literal: Literal: After this, the emperor again received feudal lords at Ganquan, and Ganquan built lodgings for them.
translations.idiomatic: After this, the emperor again received feudal lords at Ganquan, and Ganquan built lodgings for them.

sentences.zh: 勇之乃曰：「粵俗有火災，復起屋，必以大，用勝服之。」

translations.literal: Literal: Yongzhi then said: "In Yue custom, after fire disaster one must rebuild houses on a grand scale to overcome and suppress it."
translations.idiomatic: Yongzhi then said: "In Yue custom, after fire disaster one must rebuild houses on a grand scale to overcome and suppress it."

sentences.zh: 於是作建章宮，度為千門萬戶。

translations.literal: Literal: So Jianzhang Palace was built, planned as a thousand gates and ten thousand households.
translations.idiomatic: So Jianzhang Palace was built, planned as a thousand gates and ten thousand households.

sentences.zh: 前殿度高未央。

translations.literal: Literal: Its front hall was designed higher than Weiyang.
translations.idiomatic: Its front hall was designed higher than Weiyang.

sentences.zh: 其東則鳳闕，高二十餘丈。

translations.literal: Literal: To the east were Phoenix Towers, over twenty zhang high.
translations.idiomatic: To the east were Phoenix Towers, over twenty zhang high.

sentences.zh: 其西則商中，數十里虎圈。

translations.literal: Literal: To the west was Shangzhong, with tiger enclosures extending dozens of li.
translations.idiomatic: To the west was Shangzhong, with tiger enclosures extending dozens of li.

sentences.zh: 其北治大池，漸臺高二十餘丈，名曰泰液，池中有蓬萊、方丈、瀛州、壺梁，象海中神山龜魚之屬。

translations.literal: Literal: To the north a great pond was made, with the Jian Terrace over twenty zhang high, called Taiye; within the pond were Penglai, Fangzhang, Yingzhou, and Huliang, imitating sea spirit mountains and creatures such as turtles and fish.
translations.idiomatic: To the north a great pond was made, with the Jian Terrace over twenty zhang high, called Taiye; within the pond were Penglai, Fangzhang, Yingzhou, and Huliang, imitating sea spirit mountains and creatures such as turtles and fish.

sentences.zh: 其南有玉堂璧門大鳥之屬。

translations.literal: Literal: To the south were Jade Hall, Jasper Gate, and great bird features.
translations.idiomatic: To the south were Jade Hall, Jasper Gate, and great bird features.

sentences.zh: 立神明臺、井幹樓，高五十丈，輦道相屬焉。

translations.literal: Literal: The Shenming Terrace and Jinggan Tower were erected, fifty zhang high, with elevated carriage ways connecting them.
translations.idiomatic: The Shenming Terrace and Jinggan Tower were erected, fifty zhang high, with elevated carriage ways connecting them.

sentences.zh: 夏，漢改曆，以正月為歲首，而色上黃，官更印章以五字，因為太初元年。

translations.literal: Literal: In summer, Han changed the calendar, making first month the start of the year, honoring yellow as the color, and replacing official seals with five-character legends, thereby establishing first year of Taichu.
translations.idiomatic: In summer, Han changed the calendar, making first month the start of the year, honoring yellow as the color, and replacing official seals with five-character legends, thereby establishing first year of Taichu.

sentences.zh: 是歲，西伐大宛，蝗大起。

translations.literal: Literal: That year Han campaigned west against Dayuan, and locusts rose massively.
translations.idiomatic: That year Han campaigned west against Dayuan, and locusts rose massively.

sentences.zh: 丁夫人、雒陽虞初等以方祠詛匈奴、大宛焉。

translations.literal: Literal: Lady Ding and Yu Chu of Luoyang used occult rites to curse the Xiongnu and Dayuan.
translations.idiomatic: Lady Ding and Yu Chu of Luoyang used occult rites to curse the Xiongnu and Dayuan.

sentences.zh: 明年，有司言雍五畤無牢孰具，芬芳不備。

translations.literal: Literal: The next year, officials said the five Yong altars lacked fully prepared victims and complete fragrant offerings.
translations.idiomatic: The next year, officials said the five Yong altars lacked fully prepared victims and complete fragrant offerings.

sentences.zh: 乃令祠官進畤犢牢具，色食所勝，而以木寓馬代駒云。

translations.literal: Literal: So sacrificial officers were ordered to provide altar-calves and full sets, with colors and food matching conquest phases, and to use wooden model horses in place of colts.
translations.idiomatic: So sacrificial officers were ordered to provide altar-calves and full sets, with colors and food matching conquest phases, and to use wooden model horses in place of colts.

sentences.zh: 及諸名山川用駒者，悉以木寓馬代。

translations.literal: Literal: At all famous mountains and rivers where colts had been used, wooden model horses replaced them.
translations.idiomatic: At all famous mountains and rivers where colts had been used, wooden model horses replaced them.

sentences.zh: 獨行過親祠，乃用駒，它禮如故。

translations.literal: Literal: Only when the emperor passed and sacrificed in person were real colts used; other rites remained as before.
translations.idiomatic: Only when the emperor passed and sacrificed in person were real colts used; other rites remained as before.

sentences.zh: 明年，東巡海上，考神僊之屬，未有驗者。

translations.literal: Literal: The next year he toured the eastern coast and examined spirit-immortal matters; there was still no verification.
translations.idiomatic: The next year he toured the eastern coast and examined spirit-immortal matters; there was still no verification.

sentences.zh: 方士有言黃帝時為五城十二樓，以候神人於執期，名曰迎年。

translations.literal: Literal: Some fangshi said that in the Yellow Thearch's time there were five cities and twelve towers to await spirit-men at appointed seasons, called "welcoming the year."
translations.idiomatic: Some fangshi said that in the Yellow Thearch's time there were five cities and twelve towers to await spirit-men at appointed seasons, called "welcoming the year."

sentences.zh: 上許作之如方，名曰明年。

translations.literal: Literal: The emperor approved building them as prescribed and named it "Mingnian."
translations.idiomatic: The emperor approved building them as prescribed and named it "Mingnian."

sentences.zh: 上親禮祠，上犢黃焉。

translations.literal: Literal: The emperor personally performed rites and offered yellow calves.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor personally performed rites and offered yellow calves.

sentences.zh: 公玉帶曰：「黃帝時雖封泰山，然風后、封鉅、岐伯令黃帝封東泰山，禪凡山，合符，然後不死。」

translations.literal: Literal: Gongyu Dai said: "Although the Yellow Thearch performed Feng at Mount Tai, Fenghou, Fengju, and Qibo instructed him to perform Feng at East Mount Tai and Shan at Fan Mountain, matching tallies; only then did he become deathless."
translations.idiomatic: Gongyu Dai said: "Although the Yellow Thearch performed Feng at Mount Tai, Fenghou, Fengju, and Qibo instructed him to perform Feng at East Mount Tai and Shan at Fan Mountain, matching tallies; only then did he become deathless."

sentences.zh: 天子既令設祠具，至東泰山，東泰山卑小，不稱其聲，乃令祠官禮之，而不封焉。

translations.literal: Literal: After the Son of Heaven ordered ritual preparations and reached East Mount Tai, it was low and small and did not fit its reputation, so he ordered sacrificial officers to perform rites there but did not perform Feng.
translations.idiomatic: After the Son of Heaven ordered ritual preparations and reached East Mount Tai, it was low and small and did not fit its reputation, so he ordered sacrificial officers to perform rites there but did not perform Feng.

sentences.zh: 其後令帶奉祠候神物。

translations.literal: Literal: Afterward he ordered Dai to maintain sacrifice and await divine signs.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward he ordered Dai to maintain sacrifice and await divine signs.

sentences.zh: 復還泰山，修五年之禮如前，而加禪祠石閭。

translations.literal: Literal: He returned again to Mount Tai and observed the five-year rite as before, adding a Shan sacrifice at Shilu.
translations.idiomatic: He returned again to Mount Tai and observed the five-year rite as before, adding a Shan sacrifice at Shilu.

sentences.zh: 石閭者，在泰山下阯南方，方士言僊人閭也，故上親禪焉。

translations.literal: Literal: Shilu is south below Mount Tai's base; fangshi said it was a ward of immortals, so the emperor personally performed Shan there.
translations.idiomatic: Shilu is south below Mount Tai's base; fangshi said it was a ward of immortals, so the emperor personally performed Shan there.

sentences.zh: 其後五年，復至泰山修封，還過祭恆山。

translations.literal: Literal: Five years later he again came to Mount Tai for renewed Feng rites, and on return passed by and sacrificed at Mount Heng.
translations.idiomatic: Five years later he again came to Mount Tai for renewed Feng rites, and on return passed by and sacrificed at Mount Heng.

sentences.zh: 自封泰山後，十三歲而周遍於五嶽、四瀆矣。

translations.literal: Literal: From his first Feng at Mount Tai, after thirteen years he had made the full circuit of the Five Peaks and Four Waterways.
translations.idiomatic: From his first Feng at Mount Tai, after thirteen years he had made the full circuit of the Five Peaks and Four Waterways.

sentences.zh: 後五年，復至泰山修封。

translations.literal: Literal: Five years later, he again came to Mount Tai to renew Feng rites.
translations.idiomatic: Five years later, he again came to Mount Tai to renew Feng rites.

sentences.zh: 東幸琅邪，禮日成山，登之罘，浮大海，用事八神延年。

translations.literal: Literal: He went east to Langya, ritually honored the sun at Cheng Mountain, climbed Zhifu, floated on the great sea, and conducted rites to the Eight Spirits for long life.
translations.idiomatic: He went east to Langya, ritually honored the sun at Cheng Mountain, climbed Zhifu, floated on the great sea, and conducted rites to the Eight Spirits for long life.

sentences.zh: 又祠神人於交門宮，若有鄉坐拜者云。

translations.literal: Literal: He also sacrificed to spirit-men at Jiaomen Palace, where there seemed to be someone facing inward and bowing seated.
translations.idiomatic: He also sacrificed to spirit-men at Jiaomen Palace, where there seemed to be someone facing inward and bowing seated.

sentences.zh: 後五年，上復修封於泰山。

translations.literal: Literal: Five years later, the emperor again renewed Feng rites at Mount Tai.
translations.idiomatic: Five years later, the emperor again renewed Feng rites at Mount Tai.

sentences.zh: 東游東萊，臨大海。

translations.literal: Literal: He toured east to Donglai and approached the great sea.
translations.idiomatic: He toured east to Donglai and approached the great sea.

sentences.zh: 是歲，雍縣無雲如剨者三，或如虹氣蒼黃，若飛鳥集棫陽宮南，聲聞四百里。

translations.literal: Literal: That year in Yong county there were three cloudless clefts in the sky, or vapors like blue-yellow rainbow qi, like flying birds gathering south of Yuyang Palace, with sound heard four hundred li away.
translations.idiomatic: That year in Yong county there were three cloudless clefts in the sky, or vapors like blue-yellow rainbow qi, like flying birds gathering south of Yuyang Palace, with sound heard four hundred li away.

sentences.zh: 隕石二，黑如黳，有司以為美祥，以薦宗廟。

translations.literal: Literal: Two meteorites fell, black as dark dye; officials deemed them auspicious and offered them to the ancestral temple.
translations.idiomatic: Two meteorites fell, black as dark dye; officials deemed them auspicious and offered them to the ancestral temple.

sentences.zh: 而方士之候神入海求蓬萊者終無驗，公孫卿猶以大人之跡為解。

translations.literal: Literal: Yet fangshi waiting for gods and going to sea for Penglai never obtained verification; Gongsun Qing still explained it away as tracks of great beings.
translations.idiomatic: Yet fangshi waiting for gods and going to sea for Penglai never obtained verification; Gongsun Qing still explained it away as tracks of great beings.

sentences.zh: 天子猶羈縻不絕，幾遇其真。

translations.literal: Literal: The Son of Heaven still held to them without breaking off, hoping to meet the real thing.
translations.idiomatic: The Son of Heaven still held to them without breaking off, hoping to meet the real thing.

sentences.zh: 諸所興，如薄忌泰一及三一、冥羊、馬行、赤星，五床。

translations.literal: Literal: Among established cults were Bo Ji's Taiyi and Three Ones, Mingyang, Maxing, Red Star, and Five Beds.
translations.idiomatic: Among established cults were Bo Ji's Taiyi and Three Ones, Mingyang, Maxing, Red Star, and Five Beds.

sentences.zh: 寬舒之祠宮以歲時致禮。

translations.literal: Literal: At Kuan Shu's shrine-palace, rites were offered seasonally.
translations.idiomatic: At Kuan Shu's shrine-palace, rites were offered seasonally.

sentences.zh: 凡六祠，皆大祝領之。

translations.literal: Literal: In all there were six such shrines, all overseen by the Grand Invocator.
translations.idiomatic: In all there were six such shrines, all overseen by the Grand Invocator.

sentences.zh: 至如八神，諸明年、凡山它名祠，行過則祠，去則已。

translations.literal: Literal: As for the Eight Spirits, all Mingnian shrines, Fan Mountain, and other named shrines, rites were performed when passing and stopped when leaving.
translations.idiomatic: As for the Eight Spirits, all Mingnian shrines, Fan Mountain, and other named shrines, rites were performed when passing and stopped when leaving.

sentences.zh: 方士所興祠，各自主，其人終則已，祠官不主。

translations.literal: Literal: Shrines initiated by fangshi each had their own master; when that person died, the shrine ended, and sacrificial offices did not manage them.
translations.idiomatic: Shrines initiated by fangshi each had their own master; when that person died, the shrine ended, and sacrificial offices did not manage them.

sentences.zh: 它祠皆如故。

translations.literal: Literal: All other shrines remained as before.
translations.idiomatic: All other shrines remained as before.

sentences.zh: 甘泉泰一、汾陰后土，三年親郊祠，而泰山五年一修封。

translations.literal: Literal: At Ganquan-Taiyi and Fenyin-Houtu, the emperor personally performed suburban rites every three years; at Mount Tai, Feng rites were renewed every five years.
translations.idiomatic: At Ganquan-Taiyi and Fenyin-Houtu, the emperor personally performed suburban rites every three years; at Mount Tai, Feng rites were renewed every five years.

sentences.zh: 武帝凡五修封。

translations.literal: Literal: Emperor Wu renewed Feng rites five times in total.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Wu renewed Feng rites five times in total.

sentences.zh: 昭帝即位，富於春秋，未嘗親巡祭云。

translations.literal: Literal: When Emperor Zhao took the throne, he was still young and never personally made inspection sacrifices.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Zhao took the throne, he was still young and never personally made inspection sacrifices.

sentences.zh: 宣帝即位，由武帝正統興，故立三年，尊孝武廟為世宗，行所巡狩郡國皆立廟。

translations.literal: Literal: When Emperor Xuan took the throne, because he inherited Emperor Wu's legitimate line, in his third year he honored Emperor Wu's temple as Shizong, and established temples in all commanderies and kingdoms that had been visited on tours.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Xuan took the throne, because he inherited Emperor Wu's legitimate line, in his third year he honored Emperor Wu's temple as Shizong, and established temples in all commanderies and kingdoms that had been visited on tours.

sentences.zh: 告祠世宗廟日，有白鶴集後庭。

translations.literal: Literal: On the day of proclamation sacrifice at Shizong's temple, a white crane gathered in the rear court.
translations.idiomatic: On the day of proclamation sacrifice at Shizong's temple, a white crane gathered in the rear court.

sentences.zh: 以立世宗廟告祠孝昭寢，有鴈五色集殿前。

translations.literal: Literal: When announcing establishment of Shizong's temple at Emperor Zhao's mausoleum, five-colored geese gathered before the hall.
translations.idiomatic: When announcing establishment of Shizong's temple at Emperor Zhao's mausoleum, five-colored geese gathered before the hall.

sentences.zh: 西河築世宗廟，神光興於殿旁，有鳥如白鶴，前赤後青。

translations.literal: Literal: When building Shizong's temple in Xihe, divine light arose beside the hall, and a bird like a white crane appeared, red in front and blue-green behind.
translations.idiomatic: When building Shizong's temple in Xihe, divine light arose beside the hall, and a bird like a white crane appeared, red in front and blue-green behind.

sentences.zh: 神光又興於房中，如燭狀。

translations.literal: Literal: Divine light again arose within a chamber, shaped like a candle.
translations.idiomatic: Divine light again arose within a chamber, shaped like a candle.

sentences.zh: 廣川國世宗廟殿上有鍾音，門戶大開，夜有光，殿上盡明。

translations.literal: Literal: At Shizong's temple in Guangchuan state, a bell sound came from atop the hall; doors and gates flew open, and at night there was light, making the entire hall bright.
translations.idiomatic: At Shizong's temple in Guangchuan state, a bell sound came from atop the hall; doors and gates flew open, and at night there was light, making the entire hall bright.

sentences.zh: 上乃下詔赦天下。

translations.literal: Literal: The emperor then issued an amnesty for the whole realm.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor then issued an amnesty for the whole realm.

sentences.zh: 時，大將軍霍光輔政，上共己正南面，非宗廟之祀不出。

translations.literal: Literal: At this time Grand General Huo Guang assisted government; the emperor observed nonaction, properly faced south, and did not leave the palace except for ancestral rites.
translations.idiomatic: At this time Grand General Huo Guang assisted government; the emperor observed nonaction, properly faced south, and did not leave the palace except for ancestral rites.

sentences.zh: 十二年，乃下詔曰：「蓋聞天子尊事天地，修祀山川，古今通禮也。

translations.literal: Literal: In the twelfth year he issued an edict: "I have heard that for the Son of Heaven, honoring Heaven and Earth and maintaining mountain-river rites is a timeless norm of antiquity and present.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth year he issued an edict: "I have heard that for the Son of Heaven, honoring Heaven and Earth and maintaining mountain-river rites is a timeless norm of antiquity and present.

sentences.zh: 間者，上帝之祠闕而不親十有餘年，朕甚懼焉。

translations.literal: Literal: Recently, sacrifice to the High Thearch has been lacking and unattended in person for over ten years; I am deeply fearful.
translations.idiomatic: Recently, sacrifice to the High Thearch has been lacking and unattended in person for over ten years; I am deeply fearful.

sentences.zh: 朕親飭躬齊戒，親奉祀，為百姓蒙嘉氣，獲豐年焉。」

translations.literal: Literal: I have personally purified and disciplined myself and personally performed the rites, so that the people may receive auspicious qi and obtain abundant harvests."
translations.idiomatic: I have personally purified and disciplined myself and personally performed the rites, so that the people may receive auspicious qi and obtain abundant harvests."

sentences.zh: 明年正月，上始幸甘泉，郊見泰畤，數有美祥。

translations.literal: Literal: In first month of the next year, the emperor first visited Ganquan and made suburban audience at the Tai altar; auspicious signs appeared repeatedly.
translations.idiomatic: In first month of the next year, the emperor first visited Ganquan and made suburban audience at the Tai altar; auspicious signs appeared repeatedly.

sentences.zh: 修武帝故事，盛車服，敬齊祠之禮，頗作詩歌。

translations.literal: Literal: He restored Emperor Wu's precedents, made carriage and dress splendid, and reverently observed fasting and sacrificial rites, composing some hymns.
translations.idiomatic: He restored Emperor Wu's precedents, made carriage and dress splendid, and reverently observed fasting and sacrificial rites, composing some hymns.

sentences.zh: 其三月，幸河東，祠后土，有神爵集，改元為神爵。

translations.literal: Literal: That third month he visited Hedong, sacrificed to Houtu, and as divine birds gathered changed the era name to Shenjue.
translations.idiomatic: That third month he visited Hedong, sacrificed to Houtu, and as divine birds gathered changed the era name to Shenjue.

sentences.zh: 制詔太常：「夫江海，百川之大者也，今闕焉無祠。

translations.literal: Literal: He ordered the Grand Minister of Ceremonies: "The Jiang and the sea are the greatest among waters, yet now they lack rites.
translations.idiomatic: He ordered the Grand Minister of Ceremonies: "The Jiang and the sea are the greatest among waters, yet now they lack rites.

sentences.zh: 其令祠官以禮為歲事，以四時祠江海雒水，祈為天下豐年焉。」

translations.literal: Literal: Order sacrificial officials to establish annual rites by proper ritual and perform four-season sacrifices to Jiang, sea, and Luo waters, praying for harvest abundance for all under Heaven."
translations.idiomatic: Order sacrificial officials to establish annual rites by proper ritual and perform four-season sacrifices to Jiang, sea, and Luo waters, praying for harvest abundance for all under Heaven."

sentences.zh: 自是五嶽、四瀆皆有常禮。

translations.literal: Literal: From this point the Five Peaks and Four Waterways all had regular ritual schedules.
translations.idiomatic: From this point the Five Peaks and Four Waterways all had regular ritual schedules.

sentences.zh: 東嶽泰山於博，中嶽泰室於嵩高，南嶽灊山於灊，西嶽華山於華陰，北嶽常山於上曲陽，河於臨晉，江於江都，淮於平氏，濟於臨邑界中，皆使者持節侍祠。

translations.literal: Literal: Eastern Peak Mount Tai at Bo; Central Peak Taishi at Songgao; Southern Peak Qian Mountain at Qian; Western Peak Mount Hua at Huayin; Northern Peak Mount Chang at Upper Quyang; the River at Linjin; the Yangzi at Jiangdu; the Huai at Pingshi; the Ji at boundary of Linyi - all were served by envoys bearing tally.
translations.idiomatic: Eastern Peak Mount Tai at Bo; Central Peak Taishi at Songgao; Southern Peak Qian Mountain at Qian; Western Peak Mount Hua at Huayin; Northern Peak Mount Chang at Upper Quyang; the River at Linjin; the Yangzi at Jiangdu; the Huai at Pingshi; the Ji at boundary of Linyi - all were served by envoys bearing tally.

sentences.zh: 唯泰山與河歲五祠，江水四，餘皆一禱而三祠云。

translations.literal: Literal: Only Mount Tai and the River had five annual rites, Jiang had four; all others had one prayer and three rites.
translations.idiomatic: Only Mount Tai and the River had five annual rites, Jiang had four; all others had one prayer and three rites.

sentences.zh: 時，南郡獲白虎，獻其皮牙爪，上為立祠。

translations.literal: Literal: At this time Nanjun captured a white tiger and presented its skin, teeth, and claws; the emperor established a shrine for it.
translations.idiomatic: At this time Nanjun captured a white tiger and presented its skin, teeth, and claws; the emperor established a shrine for it.

sentences.zh: 又以方士言，為隨侯、劍寶、玉寶璧、周康寶鼎立四祠於未央宮中。

translations.literal: Literal: And by fangshi advice, he established four shrines in Weiyang Palace for Marquis Sui's jewel, Sword Jewel, Jade Jewel Disc, and Duke Kang's Zhou tripod.
translations.idiomatic: And by fangshi advice, he established four shrines in Weiyang Palace for Marquis Sui's jewel, Sword Jewel, Jade Jewel Disc, and Duke Kang's Zhou tripod.

sentences.zh: 又祠太室山於即墨，三戶山於下密，祠天封苑火井於鴻門。

translations.literal: Literal: He also sacrificed to Taishi Mountain at Jimo, Sanhu Mountain at Xiami, and to Heavenly-sealed Garden Fire-well at Hongmen.
translations.idiomatic: He also sacrificed to Taishi Mountain at Jimo, Sanhu Mountain at Xiami, and to Heavenly-sealed Garden Fire-well at Hongmen.

sentences.zh: 又立歲星、辰星、太白、熒惑、南斗祠於長安城旁。

translations.literal: Literal: He also established shrines for Jupiter, Mercury, Venus, Mars, and the Southern Dipper beside Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: He also established shrines for Jupiter, Mercury, Venus, Mars, and the Southern Dipper beside Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 又祠參山八神於曲城，蓬山石社石鼓於臨朐，之罘山於腄，成山於不夜，萊山於黃。

translations.literal: Literal: He also sacrificed to the Eight Spirits of Can Mountain at Qucheng, to Peng Mountain's Stone Altar and Stone Drum at Linqu, to Zhifu Mountain at Chui, to Cheng Mountain at Buye, and to Lai Mountain at Huang.
translations.idiomatic: He also sacrificed to the Eight Spirits of Can Mountain at Qucheng, to Peng Mountain's Stone Altar and Stone Drum at Linqu, to Zhifu Mountain at Chui, to Cheng Mountain at Buye, and to Lai Mountain at Huang.

sentences.zh: 成山祠日，萊山祠月。

translations.literal: Literal: At Cheng Mountain he sacrificed to the sun; at Lai Mountain to the moon.
translations.idiomatic: At Cheng Mountain he sacrificed to the sun; at Lai Mountain to the moon.

sentences.zh: 又祠四時於琅邪，蚩尤於壽良。

translations.literal: Literal: He also sacrificed to the Four Seasons at Langya and to Chiyou at Shouliang.
translations.idiomatic: He also sacrificed to the Four Seasons at Langya and to Chiyou at Shouliang.

sentences.zh: 京師近縣鄠，則有勞谷、五床山、日月、五帝、僊人、玉女祠。

translations.literal: Literal: In Hu county near the capital there were shrines to Laogu, Wuchuang Mountain, sun and moon, Five Thearchs, immortals, and jade maidens.
translations.idiomatic: In Hu county near the capital there were shrines to Laogu, Wuchuang Mountain, sun and moon, Five Thearchs, immortals, and jade maidens.

sentences.zh: 雲陽有徑路神祠，祭休屠王也。

translations.literal: Literal: At Yunyang there was a roadside spirit shrine, sacrificing to King Xiutu.
translations.idiomatic: At Yunyang there was a roadside spirit shrine, sacrificing to King Xiutu.

sentences.zh: 又立五龍山僊人祠及黃帝、天神、帝原水，凡四祠於膚施。

translations.literal: Literal: He also established at Fushi four shrines: Wulong Mountain immortal shrine, Yellow Thearch shrine, Heavenly Spirit shrine, and Diyuan Water shrine.
translations.idiomatic: He also established at Fushi four shrines: Wulong Mountain immortal shrine, Yellow Thearch shrine, Heavenly Spirit shrine, and Diyuan Water shrine.

sentences.zh: 或言益州有金馬碧雞之神，可醮祭而致，於是遣諫大夫王褒使持節而求之。

translations.literal: Literal: Someone said Yizhou had the spirits of Golden Horse and Jade Rooster, which could be brought through jiao offerings, so he sent Remonstrant Grandee Wang Bao with tally to seek them.
translations.idiomatic: Someone said Yizhou had the spirits of Golden Horse and Jade Rooster, which could be brought through jiao offerings, so he sent Remonstrant Grandee Wang Bao with tally to seek them.

sentences.zh: 大夫劉更生獻淮南枕中洪寶苑祕之方，令尚方鑄作。

translations.literal: Literal: Grandee Liu Gengsheng presented secret formulas from Huainan's Pillow-Book Great Treasure Garden and had the Imperial Workshop cast and produce them.
translations.idiomatic: Grandee Liu Gengsheng presented secret formulas from Huainan's Pillow-Book Great Treasure Garden and had the Imperial Workshop cast and produce them.

sentences.zh: 事不驗，更生坐論。

translations.literal: Literal: When they proved ineffective, Gengsheng was convicted and censured.
translations.idiomatic: When they proved ineffective, Gengsheng was convicted and censured.

sentences.zh: 京兆尹張敞上疏諫曰：「願明主時忘車馬之好，斥遠方士之虛語，游心帝王之術，太平庶幾可興也。」

translations.literal: Literal: Capital Commandant Zhang Chang memorialized in remonstrance: "May the enlightened ruler at times forget his fondness for fine chariots and horses, reject the empty talk of distant fangshi, and turn his mind to methods of ancient emperors; then great peace may perhaps be revived."
translations.idiomatic: Capital Commandant Zhang Chang memorialized in remonstrance: "May the enlightened ruler at times forget his fondness for fine chariots and horses, reject the empty talk of distant fangshi, and turn his mind to methods of ancient emperors; then great peace may perhaps be revived."

sentences.zh: 後尚方待詔皆罷。

translations.literal: Literal: Afterward all workshop consultants were dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward all workshop consultants were dismissed.

sentences.zh: 是時，美陽得鼎，獻之。

translations.literal: Literal: At this time a tripod was found at Meiyang and presented.
translations.idiomatic: At this time a tripod was found at Meiyang and presented.

sentences.zh: 下有司議，多以為宜薦見宗廟，如元鼎時故事。

translations.literal: Literal: Officials were ordered to deliberate; most said it should be presented to the ancestral temple as in the Yuanding precedent.
translations.idiomatic: Officials were ordered to deliberate; most said it should be presented to the ancestral temple as in the Yuanding precedent.

sentences.zh: 張敞好古文字，桉鼎銘勒而上議曰：「臣聞周祖始乎后稷，后稷封於斄，公劉發跡於豳，大王建國於廄梁，文武興於酆鎬。

translations.literal: Literal: Zhang Chang, skilled in ancient script, examined the tripod inscription and submitted: "I hear that Zhou ancestry began with Houji, who was enfeoffed at Tai; Gong Liu rose at Bin; Great King founded at Ju-Liang; Wen and Wu prospered at Feng and Hao.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Chang, skilled in ancient script, examined the tripod inscription and submitted: "I hear that Zhou ancestry began with Houji, who was enfeoffed at Tai; Gong Liu rose at Bin; Great King founded at Ju-Liang; Wen and Wu prospered at Feng and Hao.

sentences.zh: 由此言之，則廄梁豐鎬之間周舊居也，固宜有宗廟壇場祭祀之臧。

translations.literal: Literal: From this it follows that between Ju-Liang and Feng-Hao were old Zhou settlements where one would indeed expect temple altars and ritual deposits.
translations.idiomatic: From this it follows that between Ju-Liang and Feng-Hao were old Zhou settlements where one would indeed expect temple altars and ritual deposits.

sentences.zh: 今鼎出於廄東，中有刻書曰：『王命尸臣：「官此栒邑，賜爾旂鸞黼黻琱戈。」

translations.literal: Literal: Now this tripod emerged east of Ju, and inside is an inscription: 'The king commands minister Shi: "Administer this Xun city; I grant you banner, luan-bells, embroidered insignia, and carved halberd."
translations.idiomatic: Now this tripod emerged east of Ju, and inside is an inscription: 'The king commands minister Shi: "Administer this Xun city; I grant you banner, luan-bells, embroidered insignia, and carved halberd."

sentences.zh: 尸臣拜手稽首曰：「敢對揚天子丕顯休命。」』

translations.literal: Literal: Minister Shi bowed and knocked his head, saying: "I dare to proclaim and magnify the Son of Heaven's great and manifest gracious command."'
translations.idiomatic: Minister Shi bowed and knocked his head, saying: "I dare to proclaim and magnify the Son of Heaven's great and manifest gracious command."'

sentences.zh: 臣愚不足以跡古文，竊以傳記言之，此鼎殆周之所以褒賜大臣，大臣子孫刻銘其先功，臧之於宮廟也。

translations.literal: Literal: Though my understanding cannot fully trace ancient script, judging from transmitted records this tripod was likely a reward from Zhou to a great minister, whose descendants inscribed their ancestor's merit and stored it in palace temples.
translations.idiomatic: Though my understanding cannot fully trace ancient script, judging from transmitted records this tripod was likely a reward from Zhou to a great minister, whose descendants inscribed their ancestor's merit and stored it in palace temples.

sentences.zh: 昔寶鼎之出於汾脽也，河東太守以聞，詔曰：『朕巡祭后土，祈為百姓蒙豐年，今穀嗛未報，鼎焉為出哉？』

translations.literal: Literal: When the precious tripod emerged at Fenshui, Hedong governor reported it; the edict then said: 'I toured and sacrificed to Houtu, praying that the people receive abundant harvests. Grain has not yet repaid that prayer - why has the tripod emerged?'
translations.idiomatic: When the precious tripod emerged at Fenshui, Hedong governor reported it; the edict then said: 'I toured and sacrificed to Houtu, praying that the people receive abundant harvests. Grain has not yet repaid that prayer - why has the tripod emerged?'

sentences.zh: 博問耆老，意舊臧與？

translations.literal: Literal: He broadly questioned elders, seeking whether it had been an old deposit.
translations.idiomatic: He broadly questioned elders, seeking whether it had been an old deposit.

sentences.zh: 誠欲考得事實也。

translations.literal: Literal: He truly wished to verify facts.
translations.idiomatic: He truly wished to verify facts.

sentences.zh: 有司驗脽上非舊臧處，鼎大八尺一寸，高三尺六寸，殊異於眾鼎。

translations.literal: Literal: Officials inspected and found Fenshui was not an old deposit site; that tripod was eight chi one cun wide and three chi six cun high, very different from ordinary tripods.
translations.idiomatic: Officials inspected and found Fenshui was not an old deposit site; that tripod was eight chi one cun wide and three chi six cun high, very different from ordinary tripods.

sentences.zh: 今此鼎細小，又有款識，不宜薦見於宗廟。」

translations.literal: Literal: This present tripod is small and has inscriptions; it should not be presented in ancestral temple audience."
translations.idiomatic: This present tripod is small and has inscriptions; it should not be presented in ancestral temple audience."

sentences.zh: 制曰：「京兆尹議是。」

translations.literal: Literal: The decree said: "The Capital Commandant's judgment is correct."
translations.idiomatic: The decree said: "The Capital Commandant's judgment is correct."

sentences.zh: 上自幸河東之明年正月，鳳皇集祋祤，於所集處得玉寶，起步壽宮，乃下詔赦天下。

translations.literal: Literal: In first month of the year after the emperor personally visited Hedong, phoenixes gathered at Duiyu, and at their gathering site a jade treasure was found; Buzhou Palace was built, and an amnesty was issued.
translations.idiomatic: In first month of the year after the emperor personally visited Hedong, phoenixes gathered at Duiyu, and at their gathering site a jade treasure was found; Buzhou Palace was built, and an amnesty was issued.

sentences.zh: 後間歲，鳳皇神爵甘露降集京師，赦天下。

translations.literal: Literal: In following alternating years, phoenixes, divine birds, and sweet dew descended and gathered in the capital, and amnesties were proclaimed.
translations.idiomatic: In following alternating years, phoenixes, divine birds, and sweet dew descended and gathered in the capital, and amnesties were proclaimed.

sentences.zh: 其冬，鳳皇集上林，乃作鳳皇殿，以答嘉瑞。

translations.literal: Literal: That winter phoenixes gathered in Shanglin, so Phoenix Hall was built to respond to the auspicious omen.
translations.idiomatic: That winter phoenixes gathered in Shanglin, so Phoenix Hall was built to respond to the auspicious omen.

sentences.zh: 明年正月，復幸甘泉，郊泰畤，改元曰五鳳。

translations.literal: Literal: In first month next year, he again visited Ganquan, made suburban rites at Tai altar, and changed era name to Wufeng.
translations.idiomatic: In first month next year, he again visited Ganquan, made suburban rites at Tai altar, and changed era name to Wufeng.

sentences.zh: 明年，幸雍祠五畤。

translations.literal: Literal: The next year he visited Yong and sacrificed at the five altars.
translations.idiomatic: The next year he visited Yong and sacrificed at the five altars.

sentences.zh: 其明年春，幸河東，祠后土，赦天下。

translations.literal: Literal: In spring of the following year, he visited Hedong, sacrificed to Houtu, and granted amnesty.
translations.idiomatic: In spring of the following year, he visited Hedong, sacrificed to Houtu, and granted amnesty.

sentences.zh: 後間歲，改元為甘露。

translations.literal: Literal: In a later alternating year, the era name was changed to Ganlu.
translations.idiomatic: In a later alternating year, the era name was changed to Ganlu.

sentences.zh: 正月，上幸甘泉，郊泰畤。

translations.literal: Literal: In first month, the emperor visited Ganquan and made suburban rites at Tai altar.
translations.idiomatic: In first month, the emperor visited Ganquan and made suburban rites at Tai altar.

sentences.zh: 其夏，黃龍見新豐。

translations.literal: Literal: That summer a yellow dragon appeared at Xinfeng.
translations.idiomatic: That summer a yellow dragon appeared at Xinfeng.

sentences.zh: 建章、未央、長樂宮鍾虡銅人皆生毛，長一寸所，時以為美祥。

translations.literal: Literal: Bronze figures on the bell-racks of Jianzhang, Weiyang, and Changle palaces all grew hair about one cun long, then regarded as auspicious.
translations.idiomatic: Bronze figures on the bell-racks of Jianzhang, Weiyang, and Changle palaces all grew hair about one cun long, then regarded as auspicious.

sentences.zh: 後間歲正月，上郊泰畤，因朝單于於甘泉宮。

translations.literal: Literal: In a later alternating year's first month, the emperor sacrificed at Tai altar and then received audience from the Chanyu at Ganquan Palace.
translations.idiomatic: In a later alternating year's first month, the emperor sacrificed at Tai altar and then received audience from the Chanyu at Ganquan Palace.

sentences.zh: 後間歲，改元為黃龍。

translations.literal: Literal: In another alternating year, the era was changed to Huanglong.
translations.idiomatic: In another alternating year, the era was changed to Huanglong.

sentences.zh: 正月，復幸甘泉，郊泰畤，又朝單于於甘泉宮。

translations.literal: Literal: In first month he again visited Ganquan, sacrificed at Tai altar, and again received the Chanyu there.
translations.idiomatic: In first month he again visited Ganquan, sacrificed at Tai altar, and again received the Chanyu there.

sentences.zh: 至冬而崩。

translations.literal: Literal: By winter he died.
translations.idiomatic: By winter he died.

sentences.zh: 鳳皇下郡國凡五十餘所。

translations.literal: Literal: Phoenixes descended in commanderies and kingdoms at over fifty sites.
translations.idiomatic: Phoenixes descended in commanderies and kingdoms at over fifty sites.

sentences.zh: 元帝即位，遵舊儀，間歲正月，一幸甘泉郊泰畤，又東至河東祠后土，西至雍祠五畤。

translations.literal: Literal: When Emperor Yuan took the throne, he followed old ritual: in alternating years, first month, he once visited Ganquan for suburban Tai rites, then east to Hedong for Houtu and west to Yong for five altars.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Yuan took the throne, he followed old ritual: in alternating years, first month, he once visited Ganquan for suburban Tai rites, then east to Hedong for Houtu and west to Yong for five altars.

sentences.zh: 凡五奉泰畤、后土之祠。

translations.literal: Literal: In all, he personally performed Tai and Houtu rites five times.
translations.idiomatic: In all, he personally performed Tai and Houtu rites five times.

sentences.zh: 亦施恩澤，時所過毋出田租，賜百戶牛酒，或賜爵，赦罪人。

translations.literal: Literal: He also bestowed favors: where he passed, that year's land tax was remitted; one hundred households were granted oxen and wine; sometimes ranks were granted and prisoners pardoned.
translations.idiomatic: He also bestowed favors: where he passed, that year's land tax was remitted; one hundred households were granted oxen and wine; sometimes ranks were granted and prisoners pardoned.

sentences.zh: 元帝好儒，貢禹、韋玄成、匡衡等相繼為公卿。

translations.literal: Literal: Emperor Yuan favored Confucian learning, and Gong Yu, Wei Xuancheng, Kuang Heng, and others successively became high ministers.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Yuan favored Confucian learning, and Gong Yu, Wei Xuancheng, Kuang Heng, and others successively became high ministers.

sentences.zh: 禹建言漢家宗廟祭祀多不應古禮，上是其言。

translations.literal: Literal: Yu argued that many Han ancestral rites did not accord with ancient ritual, and the emperor accepted his view.
translations.idiomatic: Yu argued that many Han ancestral rites did not accord with ancient ritual, and the emperor accepted his view.

sentences.zh: 後韋玄成為丞相，議罷郡國廟，自太上皇、孝惠帝諸園寢廟皆罷。

translations.literal: Literal: Later, when Wei Xuancheng became chancellor, it was decided to abolish commandery and kingdom temples; from the Supreme Emperor and Emperor Hui onward, mausoleum-park temples were all abolished.
translations.idiomatic: Later, when Wei Xuancheng became chancellor, it was decided to abolish commandery and kingdom temples; from the Supreme Emperor and Emperor Hui onward, mausoleum-park temples were all abolished.

sentences.zh: 後元帝寢疾，夢神靈譴罷諸廟祠，上遂復焉。

translations.literal: Literal: Later, when Emperor Yuan was gravely ill, he dreamed of spirits reproaching abolition of the temples, and so restored them.
translations.idiomatic: Later, when Emperor Yuan was gravely ill, he dreamed of spirits reproaching abolition of the temples, and so restored them.

sentences.zh: 後或罷或復，至哀、平不定。

translations.literal: Literal: Afterward some were abolished and some restored; through Ai and Ping it remained unsettled.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward some were abolished and some restored; through Ai and Ping it remained unsettled.

sentences.zh: 語在韋玄成傳。

translations.literal: Literal: This is recorded in Wei Xuancheng's biography.
translations.idiomatic: This is recorded in Wei Xuancheng's biography.

sentences.zh: 成帝初即位，丞相衡、御史大夫譚奏言：「帝王之事莫大乎承天之序，承天之序莫重於郊祀，故聖王盡心極慮以建其制。

translations.literal: Literal: When Emperor Cheng first took the throne, Chancellor Heng and Censor-in-Chief Tan memorialized: "Among imperial tasks none is greater than continuing Heaven's order; and in continuing Heaven's order, none is weightier than suburban sacrifice. Therefore sage kings exhausted mind and thought to establish its regulations.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Cheng first took the throne, Chancellor Heng and Censor-in-Chief Tan memorialized: "Among imperial tasks none is greater than continuing Heaven's order; and in continuing Heaven's order, none is weightier than suburban sacrifice. Therefore sage kings exhausted mind and thought to establish its regulations.

sentences.zh: 祭天於南郊，就陽之義也；

translations.literal: Literal: Sacrificing to Heaven in the southern suburb accords with pursuing yang.
translations.idiomatic: Sacrificing to Heaven in the southern suburb accords with pursuing yang.

sentences.zh: 瘞地於北郊，即陰之象也。

translations.literal: Literal: Burying offerings to Earth in the northern suburb accords with the image of yin.
translations.idiomatic: Burying offerings to Earth in the northern suburb accords with the image of yin.

sentences.zh: 天之於天子也，因其所都而各饗焉。

translations.literal: Literal: For Heaven in relation to the Son of Heaven, wherever he establishes his capital, there Heaven receives offerings there.
translations.idiomatic: For Heaven in relation to the Son of Heaven, wherever he establishes his capital, there Heaven receives offerings there.

sentences.zh: 往者，孝武皇帝居甘泉宮，即於雲陽立泰畤，祭於宮南。

translations.literal: Literal: Formerly, Emperor Wu resided at Ganquan Palace and so established the Tai altar at Yunyang, sacrificing south of the palace.
translations.idiomatic: Formerly, Emperor Wu resided at Ganquan Palace and so established the Tai altar at Yunyang, sacrificing south of the palace.

sentences.zh: 今行常幸長安，郊見皇天反北之泰陰，祠后土反東之少陽，事與古制殊。

translations.literal: Literal: Now with regular residence in Chang'an, to make suburban audience to August Heaven at northern Taiyin and sacrifice Houtu at eastern Shaoyang differs from ancient system.
translations.idiomatic: Now with regular residence in Chang'an, to make suburban audience to August Heaven at northern Taiyin and sacrifice Houtu at eastern Shaoyang differs from ancient system.

sentences.zh: 又至雲陽，行谿谷中，阨陝且百里，汾陰則渡大川，有風波舟楫之危，皆非聖主所宜數乘。

translations.literal: Literal: Moreover, going to Yunyang requires travel through valley routes, narrow and constricted for nearly a hundred li; at Fenyin one must cross great waters with danger from wind, waves, and boats - none fit for a sage ruler to undertake often.
translations.idiomatic: Moreover, going to Yunyang requires travel through valley routes, narrow and constricted for nearly a hundred li; at Fenyin one must cross great waters with danger from wind, waves, and boats - none fit for a sage ruler to undertake often.

sentences.zh: 郡縣治道共張，吏民困苦，百官煩費。

translations.literal: Literal: Commanderies and counties must repair roads and prepare supplies; officials and people are burdened, and all offices are vexed with expense.
translations.idiomatic: Commanderies and counties must repair roads and prepare supplies; officials and people are burdened, and all offices are vexed with expense.

sentences.zh: 勞所保之民，行危險之地，難以奉神靈而祈福祐，殆未合於承天子民之意。

translations.literal: Literal: To weary the people one is meant to protect and travel dangerous ground, while seeking divine blessing, hardly accords with the intent of bearing Heaven and caring for the people.
translations.idiomatic: To weary the people one is meant to protect and travel dangerous ground, while seeking divine blessing, hardly accords with the intent of bearing Heaven and caring for the people.

sentences.zh: 昔者周文武郊於豐鄗，成王郊於雒邑。

translations.literal: Literal: In former times Zhou Wen and Wu made suburban sacrifice at Feng-Hao; King Cheng made it at Luoyi.
translations.idiomatic: In former times Zhou Wen and Wu made suburban sacrifice at Feng-Hao; King Cheng made it at Luoyi.

sentences.zh: 由此觀之，天隨王者所居而饗之，可見也。

translations.literal: Literal: From this it is clear: Heaven receives offerings where the ruler resides.
translations.idiomatic: From this it is clear: Heaven receives offerings where the ruler resides.

sentences.zh: 甘泉泰畤、河東后土之祠宜可徙置長安，合於古帝王。

translations.literal: Literal: The Ganquan Tai altar and Hedong Houtu shrine should be moved to Chang'an, in accord with ancient emperors.
translations.idiomatic: The Ganquan Tai altar and Hedong Houtu shrine should be moved to Chang'an, in accord with ancient emperors.

sentences.zh: 願與群臣議定。」

translations.literal: Literal: We request deliberation with all ministers to settle this."
translations.idiomatic: We request deliberation with all ministers to settle this."

sentences.zh: 奏可。

translations.literal: Literal: The memorial was approved.
translations.idiomatic: The memorial was approved.

sentences.zh: 大司馬車騎將軍許嘉等八人以為所從來久遠，宜如故。

translations.literal: Literal: Grand Marshal, Chariot-Cavalry General Xu Jia and seven others argued that longstanding precedent should remain unchanged.
translations.idiomatic: Grand Marshal, Chariot-Cavalry General Xu Jia and seven others argued that longstanding precedent should remain unchanged.

sentences.zh: 右將軍王商、博士師丹、議郎翟方進等五十人以為禮記曰「燔柴於太壇，祭天也；

translations.literal: Literal: Right General Wang Shang, Erudite Shi Dan, Consulting Gentleman Zhai Fangjin and fifty others argued: "The Liji says: 'Burning wood on the great altar is sacrifice to Heaven;
translations.idiomatic: Right General Wang Shang, Erudite Shi Dan, Consulting Gentleman Zhai Fangjin and fifty others argued: "The Liji says: 'Burning wood on the great altar is sacrifice to Heaven;

sentences.zh: 瘞薶於大折，祭地也。」

translations.literal: Literal: burying offerings at the great pit is sacrifice to Earth.'
translations.idiomatic: burying offerings at the great pit is sacrifice to Earth.'

sentences.zh: 兆於南郊，所以定天位也。

translations.literal: Literal: The southern-suburb ritual marker fixes Heaven's position.
translations.idiomatic: The southern-suburb ritual marker fixes Heaven's position.

sentences.zh: 祭地於大折，在北郊，就陰位也。

translations.literal: Literal: Sacrificing Earth at the great pit in the northern suburb accords with yin position.
translations.idiomatic: Sacrificing Earth at the great pit in the northern suburb accords with yin position.

sentences.zh: 郊處各在聖王所都之南北。

translations.literal: Literal: Each suburb belongs south and north of the capital where sage kings reside.
translations.idiomatic: Each suburb belongs south and north of the capital where sage kings reside.

sentences.zh: 書曰「越三日丁巳，用牲於郊，牛二。」

translations.literal: Literal: The Book says: 'Three days later, on ding-si, use two oxen at the suburban rite.'
translations.idiomatic: The Book says: 'Three days later, on ding-si, use two oxen at the suburban rite.'

sentences.zh: 周公加牲，告徙新邑，定郊禮於雒。

translations.literal: Literal: The Duke of Zhou added victims when announcing relocation to the new city and established suburban rites at Luo.
translations.idiomatic: The Duke of Zhou added victims when announcing relocation to the new city and established suburban rites at Luo.

sentences.zh: 明王聖主，事天明，事地察。

translations.literal: Literal: An enlightened sage ruler serves Heaven with clarity and serves Earth with careful discernment.
translations.idiomatic: An enlightened sage ruler serves Heaven with clarity and serves Earth with careful discernment.

sentences.zh: 天地明察，神明章矣。

translations.literal: Literal: When Heaven and Earth are served with clarity and discernment, numinous powers are made manifest.
translations.idiomatic: When Heaven and Earth are served with clarity and discernment, numinous powers are made manifest.

sentences.zh: 天地以王者為主，故聖王制祭天地之禮必於國郊。

translations.literal: Literal: Heaven and Earth take the ruler as principal; thus sage kings set Heaven-Earth sacrifice necessarily at the state suburbs.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven and Earth take the ruler as principal; thus sage kings set Heaven-Earth sacrifice necessarily at the state suburbs.

sentences.zh: 長安，聖主之居，皇天所觀視也。

translations.literal: Literal: Chang'an is the dwelling of the sage ruler and where August Heaven looks down.
translations.idiomatic: Chang'an is the dwelling of the sage ruler and where August Heaven looks down.

sentences.zh: 甘泉、河東之祠非神靈所饗，宜徙就正陽大陰之處。

translations.literal: Literal: The Ganquan and Hedong rites are not where spirits properly receive offerings and should be moved to correct yang and great yin positions.
translations.idiomatic: The Ganquan and Hedong rites are not where spirits properly receive offerings and should be moved to correct yang and great yin positions.

sentences.zh: 違俗復古，循聖制，定天位，如禮便。

translations.literal: Literal: To depart from current custom and restore antiquity, following sage institutions and fixing Heaven's position, is ritually convenient."
translations.idiomatic: To depart from current custom and restore antiquity, following sage institutions and fixing Heaven's position, is ritually convenient."

sentences.zh: 於是衡、譚奏議曰：「陛下聖德，璴明上通，承天之大，典覽群下，使各悉心盡慮，議郊祀之處，天下幸甚。

translations.literal: Literal: Thereupon Heng and Tan submitted further: "Your Majesty's sagely virtue and bright insight reach upward; you have undertaken Heaven's great charge, examined all below, and had each devote full thought to the location of suburban rites - great fortune for the realm.
translations.idiomatic: Thereupon Heng and Tan submitted further: "Your Majesty's sagely virtue and bright insight reach upward; you have undertaken Heaven's great charge, examined all below, and had each devote full thought to the location of suburban rites - great fortune for the realm.

sentences.zh: 臣聞廣謀從眾，則合於天心，故洪範曰『三人占，則從二人言』，言少從多之義也。

translations.literal: Literal: I have heard that broad planning and following the many accords with Heaven's mind; therefore the Hongfan says, 'If three divine, follow what two say,' meaning that fewer should follow the many.
translations.idiomatic: I have heard that broad planning and following the many accords with Heaven's mind; therefore the Hongfan says, 'If three divine, follow what two say,' meaning that fewer should follow the many.

sentences.zh: 論當往古，宜於萬民，則依而從之；

translations.literal: Literal: If a proposal accords with antiquity and suits the people, it should be followed.
translations.idiomatic: If a proposal accords with antiquity and suits the people, it should be followed.

sentences.zh: 違道寡與，則廢而不行。

translations.literal: Literal: If it departs from the Way and has little support, it should be discarded.
translations.idiomatic: If it departs from the Way and has little support, it should be discarded.

sentences.zh: 今議者五十八人，其五十人言當徙之義，皆著於經傳，同於上世，便於吏民；

translations.literal: Literal: Now among fifty-eight discussants, fifty support relocation, all grounded in canonical transmission, matching earlier ages, and convenient for officials and people.
translations.idiomatic: Now among fifty-eight discussants, fifty support relocation, all grounded in canonical transmission, matching earlier ages, and convenient for officials and people.

sentences.zh: 八人不案經藝，考古制，而以為不宜，無法之議，難以定吉凶。

translations.literal: Literal: The eight dissenters did not base themselves on classics or examine ancient institutions; their argument of no change has no legal principle and cannot determine auspice or inauspice.
translations.idiomatic: The eight dissenters did not base themselves on classics or examine ancient institutions; their argument of no change has no legal principle and cannot determine auspice or inauspice.

sentences.zh: 太誓曰：『正稽古立功立事，可以永年，丕天之大律。』

translations.literal: Literal: The Taishi says: 'Correctly examining antiquity to establish merit and institutions can make years enduring; this is Heaven's great law.'"
translations.idiomatic: The Taishi says: 'Correctly examining antiquity to establish merit and institutions can make years enduring; this is Heaven's great law.'"

sentences.zh: 《詩》曰『毋曰高高在上，陟降厥士，日監在茲』，言天之日監王者之處也。

translations.literal: Literal: The Odes says, "Do not say Heaven is high above; it ascends and descends among its officers, watching here each day," meaning Heaven daily watches where rulers dwell.
translations.idiomatic: The Odes says, "Do not say Heaven is high above; it ascends and descends among its officers, watching here each day," meaning Heaven daily watches where rulers dwell.

sentences.zh: 又曰『乃眷西顧，此維予宅』，言天以文王之都為居也。

translations.literal: Literal: It also says, "Then he looked back westward: this is my dwelling," meaning Heaven took King Wen's capital as its dwelling.
translations.idiomatic: It also says, "Then he looked back westward: this is my dwelling," meaning Heaven took King Wen's capital as its dwelling.

sentences.zh: 宜於長安定南北郊，為萬世基。」

translations.literal: Literal: Thus southern and northern suburbs should be fixed at Chang'an as the foundation for ten thousand generations."
translations.idiomatic: Thus southern and northern suburbs should be fixed at Chang'an as the foundation for ten thousand generations."

sentences.zh: 天子從之。

translations.literal: Literal: The Son of Heaven followed this.
translations.idiomatic: The Son of Heaven followed this.

sentences.zh: 既定，衡言：「甘泉泰畤紫壇，八觚宣通象八方。

translations.literal: Literal: After it was settled, Heng said: "At Ganquan, the Tai altar's purple platform has eight corners opening through, symbolizing eight directions.
translations.idiomatic: After it was settled, Heng said: "At Ganquan, the Tai altar's purple platform has eight corners opening through, symbolizing eight directions.

sentences.zh: 五帝壇周環其下，又有群神之壇。

translations.literal: Literal: Below it the Five Thearch altars encircle it, and there are also altars for many spirits.
translations.idiomatic: Below it the Five Thearch altars encircle it, and there are also altars for many spirits.

sentences.zh: 以尚書禋六宗、望山川、遍群神之義，紫壇有文章采鏤黼黻之飾及玉、女樂，石壇、僊人祠，瘞鸞路、騂駒、寓龍馬，不能得其象於古。

translations.literal: Literal: According to Shangshu's meaning of yin sacrifice to six powers, distant offerings to mountains and rivers, and complete offerings to all spirits, the purple altar has carved ornament, patterned embroidery, jade, female music, stone altars, and immortal shrines, and buried luan chariots, red colts, and model dragon-horses - none matching ancient models.
translations.idiomatic: According to Shangshu's meaning of yin sacrifice to six powers, distant offerings to mountains and rivers, and complete offerings to all spirits, the purple altar has carved ornament, patterned embroidery, jade, female music, stone altars, and immortal shrines, and buried luan chariots, red colts, and model dragon-horses - none matching ancient models.

sentences.zh: 臣聞郊紫壇饗帝之義，埽地而祭，上質也。

translations.literal: Literal: I have heard that the suburban purple-altar rite to feast the Thearch should be sweeping the ground and sacrificing, valuing plain substance.
translations.idiomatic: I have heard that the suburban purple-altar rite to feast the Thearch should be sweeping the ground and sacrificing, valuing plain substance.

sentences.zh: 歌大呂舞雲門以俟天神，歌太蔟舞咸池以俟地祇，其牲用犢，其席槁峵，其器陶匏，皆因天地之性，貴誠上質，不敢修其文也。

translations.literal: Literal: One sings Dalu and dances Yunmen to await heavenly gods; sings Taicu and dances Xianchi to await earthly powers; uses calves as victims, coarse grass mats, pottery and gourd vessels - all according to Heaven-Earth nature, honoring sincerity and plainness and not daring ornate embellishment.
translations.idiomatic: One sings Dalu and dances Yunmen to await heavenly gods; sings Taicu and dances Xianchi to await earthly powers; uses calves as victims, coarse grass mats, pottery and gourd vessels - all according to Heaven-Earth nature, honoring sincerity and plainness and not daring ornate embellishment.

sentences.zh: 以為神祇功德至大，雖修精微而備庶物，猶不足以報功，唯至誠為可，致上質不飾，以章天德。

translations.literal: Literal: Because the merits of gods and powers are supremely great, even refined preparations of all things are still insufficient to repay them; only utmost sincerity suffices, so plain unornamented ritual manifests Heaven's virtue.
translations.idiomatic: Because the merits of gods and powers are supremely great, even refined preparations of all things are still insufficient to repay them; only utmost sincerity suffices, so plain unornamented ritual manifests Heaven's virtue.

sentences.zh: 紫壇偽飾女樂、鸞路、騂駒、龍馬、石壇之屬，宜皆勿修。」

translations.literal: Literal: Thus false adornments such as female music, luan chariots, red colts, dragon-horses, and stone altars at the purple platform should all cease."
translations.idiomatic: Thus false adornments such as female music, luan chariots, red colts, dragon-horses, and stone altars at the purple platform should all cease."

sentences.zh: 衡又言：「王者各以其禮制事天地，非因異世所立而繼之。

translations.literal: Literal: Heng also said: "Each kingly house uses its own ritual system for Heaven and Earth, not simply inheriting what different ages established.
translations.idiomatic: Heng also said: "Each kingly house uses its own ritual system for Heaven and Earth, not simply inheriting what different ages established.

sentences.zh: 今雍鄜、密、上下畤，本秦侯各以其意所立，非禮之所載術也。

translations.literal: Literal: The Yong Fu, Mi, and Upper-Lower altars were originally established by Qin marquises according to their own ideas, not by procedures contained in ritual canons.
translations.idiomatic: The Yong Fu, Mi, and Upper-Lower altars were originally established by Qin marquises according to their own ideas, not by procedures contained in ritual canons.

sentences.zh: 漢興之初，儀制未及定，即且因秦故祠，復立北畤。

translations.literal: Literal: At Han's beginning, ritual institutions were not yet fixed, so it temporarily followed Qin rites and additionally set up Northern altar.
translations.idiomatic: At Han's beginning, ritual institutions were not yet fixed, so it temporarily followed Qin rites and additionally set up Northern altar.

sentences.zh: 今既稽古，建定天地之大禮，郊見上帝，青赤白黃黑五方之帝皆畢陳，各有位饌，祭祀備具。

translations.literal: Literal: Now that antiquity has been examined and the great Heaven-Earth rites fixed - with suburban audience to High Thearch and full arrangement of the five directional Thearchs in blue, red, white, yellow, and black, each with its proper seat and offerings - the sacrifices are fully complete.
translations.idiomatic: Now that antiquity has been examined and the great Heaven-Earth rites fixed - with suburban audience to High Thearch and full arrangement of the five directional Thearchs in blue, red, white, yellow, and black, each with its proper seat and offerings - the sacrifices are fully complete.

sentences.zh: 諸侯所妄造，王者不當長遵。

translations.literal: Literal: What feudal lords arbitrarily created should not be followed long by a kingly house.
translations.idiomatic: What feudal lords arbitrarily created should not be followed long by a kingly house.

sentences.zh: 及北畤，未定時所立，不宜復修。」

translations.literal: Literal: As for Northern altar, it was set up before institutions were fixed and should not be maintained."
translations.idiomatic: As for Northern altar, it was set up before institutions were fixed and should not be maintained."

sentences.zh: 天子皆從焉。

translations.literal: Literal: The Son of Heaven accepted all this.
translations.idiomatic: The Son of Heaven accepted all this.

sentences.zh: 及陳寶祠，由是皆罷。

translations.literal: Literal: From then on, including Chenbao shrine, all were abolished.
translations.idiomatic: From then on, including Chenbao shrine, all were abolished.

sentences.zh: 明年，上始祀南郊，赦奉郊之縣及中都官耐罪囚徒。

translations.literal: Literal: The next year the emperor first sacrificed at southern suburb and pardoned penal convicts in counties serving suburban rites and in central offices.
translations.idiomatic: The next year the emperor first sacrificed at southern suburb and pardoned penal convicts in counties serving suburban rites and in central offices.

sentences.zh: 是歲衡、譚復條奏：「長安廚官縣官給祠郡國候神方士使者所祠，凡六百八十三所，其二百八所應禮，及疑無明文，可奉祠如故。

translations.literal: Literal: That year Heng and Tan again submitted itemized memorial: "Chang'an culinary offices and county offices provide for sacrifices of commanderies, kingdoms, spirit-waiting fangshi, and envoys, totaling 683 sites. Of these, 208 accord with ritual, and some others lacking clear texts may continue as before.
translations.idiomatic: That year Heng and Tan again submitted itemized memorial: "Chang'an culinary offices and county offices provide for sacrifices of commanderies, kingdoms, spirit-waiting fangshi, and envoys, totaling 683 sites. Of these, 208 accord with ritual, and some others lacking clear texts may continue as before.

sentences.zh: 其餘四百七十五所不應禮，或復重，請皆罷。」

translations.literal: Literal: The remaining 475 do not accord with ritual or are duplicative; we request all be abolished."
translations.idiomatic: The remaining 475 do not accord with ritual or are duplicative; we request all be abolished."

sentences.zh: 奏可。

translations.literal: Literal: The memorial was approved.
translations.idiomatic: The memorial was approved.

sentences.zh: 本雍舊祠二百三所，唯山川諸星十五所為應禮云。

translations.literal: Literal: Of the old Yong shrines, 203 in total, only fifteen for mountains, rivers, and stars were deemed ritually proper.
translations.idiomatic: Of the old Yong shrines, 203 in total, only fifteen for mountains, rivers, and stars were deemed ritually proper.

sentences.zh: 若諸布、諸嚴、諸逐，皆罷。

translations.literal: Literal: Sites such as various Bu, Yan, and Zhu rites were all abolished.
translations.idiomatic: Sites such as various Bu, Yan, and Zhu rites were all abolished.

sentences.zh: 杜主有五祠，置其一。

translations.literal: Literal: Of five Du Lord shrines, only one was kept.
translations.idiomatic: Of five Du Lord shrines, only one was kept.

sentences.zh: 又罷高祖所立梁、晉、秦、荊巫、九天、南山、萊中之屬，及孝文渭陽、孝武薄忌泰一、三一、黃帝、冥羊、馬行、泰一、皋山山君、武夷、夏后啟母石、萬里沙、八神、延年之屬，及孝宣參山、蓬山、之罘、成山、萊山、四時、蚩尤、勞谷、五床、僊人、玉女、徑路、黃帝、天神、原水之屬，皆罷。

translations.literal: Literal: Also abolished were Gaozu's Liang, Jin, Qin, Chu shamans, Nine Heavens, Nanshan, Laizhong rites; and Emperor Wen's Weiyang rites; Emperor Wu's Bo Ji Taiyi, Three Ones, Yellow Thearch, Mingyang, Maxing, Taiyi, Gaoshan Lord, Wuyi, Xia Hou Qi's mother stone, Wanli Sha, Eight Spirits, Yannian rites; and Emperor Xuan's Can Mountain, Peng Mountain, Zhifu, Cheng Mountain, Lai Mountain, Four Seasons, Chiyou, Laogu, Five Beds, immortals, jade maidens, Jinglu, Yellow Thearch, Heavenly Spirit, Yuanshui rites - all abolished.
translations.idiomatic: Also abolished were Gaozu's Liang, Jin, Qin, Chu shamans, Nine Heavens, Nanshan, Laizhong rites; and Emperor Wen's Weiyang rites; Emperor Wu's Bo Ji Taiyi, Three Ones, Yellow Thearch, Mingyang, Maxing, Taiyi, Gaoshan Lord, Wuyi, Xia Hou Qi's mother stone, Wanli Sha, Eight Spirits, Yannian rites; and Emperor Xuan's Can Mountain, Peng Mountain, Zhifu, Cheng Mountain, Lai Mountain, Four Seasons, Chiyou, Laogu, Five Beds, immortals, jade maidens, Jinglu, Yellow Thearch, Heavenly Spirit, Yuanshui rites - all abolished.

sentences.zh: 候神方士使者副佐、本草待詔七十餘人皆歸家。

translations.literal: Literal: Over seventy assistant spirit-waiting fangshi envoys and materia-medica consultants were all sent home.
translations.idiomatic: Over seventy assistant spirit-waiting fangshi envoys and materia-medica consultants were all sent home.

sentences.zh: 誣神者殃及三世。』

translations.literal: Literal: "Those who deceive through spirits bring disaster on three generations."
translations.idiomatic: "Those who deceive through spirits bring disaster on three generations."

sentences.zh: 恐其咎不獨止禹等。」

translations.literal: Literal: He feared their blame might not stop with Yu and others alone.
translations.idiomatic: He feared their blame might not stop with Yu and others alone.

sentences.zh: 上意恨之。

translations.literal: Literal: The emperor inwardly resented this.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor inwardly resented this.

sentences.zh: 後上以無繼嗣故，今皇太后詔有司曰：「蓋聞王者承事天地，交接泰一，尊莫著於祭祀。

translations.literal: Literal: Later, because the emperor had no heir, the Empress Dowager issued edict through officials: "I hear that among kingly duties to Heaven and Earth and exchange with Taiyi, nothing is more manifestly honored than sacrifice.
translations.idiomatic: Later, because the emperor had no heir, the Empress Dowager issued edict through officials: "I hear that among kingly duties to Heaven and Earth and exchange with Taiyi, nothing is more manifestly honored than sacrifice.

sentences.zh: 孝武皇帝大聖通明，始建上下之祀，營泰畤於甘泉，定后土於汾陰，而神祇安之，饗國長久，子孫蕃滋，累世遵業，福流於今。

translations.literal: Literal: Emperor Wu, great sage and clear in penetration, first established upper and lower rites, set the Tai altar at Ganquan, fixed Houtu at Fenyin; gods and powers accepted this, granting long state duration and flourishing descendants through successive generations, with blessing flowing to today.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Wu, great sage and clear in penetration, first established upper and lower rites, set the Tai altar at Ganquan, fixed Houtu at Fenyin; gods and powers accepted this, granting long state duration and flourishing descendants through successive generations, with blessing flowing to today.

sentences.zh: 今皇帝寬仁孝順，奉循聖緒，靡有大愆，而久無繼嗣。

translations.literal: Literal: Now the emperor is broad-hearted, benevolent, and filial, following sacred succession without grave fault, yet long without heir.
translations.idiomatic: Now the emperor is broad-hearted, benevolent, and filial, following sacred succession without grave fault, yet long without heir.

sentences.zh: 思其咎職，殆在徙南北郊，違先帝之制，改神祇舊位，失天地之心，以妨繼嗣之福。
