translations.literal: Literal: Reflecting on where blame lies, it is likely in moving southern and northern suburbs, departing from former emperor's institutions, changing old seats of divine powers, losing Heaven-Earth favor, and obstructing the blessing of succession.
translations.idiomatic: Reflecting on where blame lies, it is likely in moving southern and northern suburbs, departing from former emperor's institutions, changing old seats of divine powers, losing Heaven-Earth favor, and obstructing the blessing of succession.

sentences.zh: 春秋六十，未見皇孫，食不甘味，寢不安席，朕甚悼焉。

translations.literal: Literal: At sixty years old I still have not seen an imperial grandson; food has no flavor, sleep no rest, and I grieve deeply.
translations.idiomatic: At sixty years old I still have not seen an imperial grandson; food has no flavor, sleep no rest, and I grieve deeply.

sentences.zh: 春秋大復古，善順祀。

translations.literal: Literal: The Spring and Autumn greatly values restoring antiquity and rightly aligning sacrifice.
translations.idiomatic: The Spring and Autumn greatly values restoring antiquity and rightly aligning sacrifice.

sentences.zh: 其復甘泉泰畤，汾陰后土如故，及雍五畤、陳寶祠在陳倉者。」

translations.literal: Literal: Restore as before Ganquan Tai altar, Fenyin Houtu, and Yong five altars, and Chenbao shrine at Chencang."
translations.idiomatic: Restore as before Ganquan Tai altar, Fenyin Houtu, and Yong five altars, and Chenbao shrine at Chencang."

sentences.zh: 天子復親郊禮如前。

translations.literal: Literal: The Son of Heaven again personally performed suburban rites as before.
translations.idiomatic: The Son of Heaven again personally performed suburban rites as before.

sentences.zh: 又復長安、雍及郡國祠著明者且半。

translations.literal: Literal: And roughly half the notable shrines of Chang'an, Yong, and commanderies were also restored.
translations.idiomatic: And roughly half the notable shrines of Chang'an, Yong, and commanderies were also restored.

sentences.zh: 成帝末年頗好鬼神，亦以無繼嗣故，多上書言祭祀方術者，皆得待詔，祠祭上林苑中長安城旁，費用甚多，然無大貴盛者，谷永說上曰：「臣聞明於天地之性，不可或以神怪；

translations.literal: Literal: In late Emperor Cheng's reign he became somewhat fond of spirits and, also because of no heir, many memorials on sacrificial techniques were accepted; their proponents were made consultants and held rites in Shanglin Park and beside Chang'an, with huge expense, yet none achieved great success. Gu Yong advised the emperor: "I hear that one who understands Heaven-Earth nature cannot be swayed by spirit prodigies;
translations.idiomatic: In late Emperor Cheng's reign he became somewhat fond of spirits and, also because of no heir, many memorials on sacrificial techniques were accepted; their proponents were made consultants and held rites in Shanglin Park and beside Chang'an, with huge expense, yet none achieved great success. Gu Yong advised the emperor: "I hear that one who understands Heaven-Earth nature cannot be swayed by spirit prodigies;

sentences.zh: 知萬物之情，不可罔以非類。

translations.literal: Literal: one who understands the true condition of things cannot be deceived by what is not of its kind.
translations.idiomatic: one who understands the true condition of things cannot be deceived by what is not of its kind.

sentences.zh: 諸背仁義之正道，不遵五經之法言，而盛稱奇怪鬼神，廣崇祭祀之方，求報無福之祠，及言世有僊人，服食不終之藥，揽興輕舉，登遐倒景，覽觀縣圃，浮游蓬萊，耕耘五德，朝種暮穫，與山石無極，黃冶變化，堅冰淖溺，化色五倉之術者，皆姦人惑眾，挾左道，懷詐偽，以欺罔世主。

translations.literal: Literal: All who depart from the right way of benevolence and righteousness, ignore the normative words of the Five Classics, and loudly proclaim strange ghosts and spirits, vastly promoting ritual techniques, seeking reward from unblessed shrines, and saying there are immortals, life-ending medicines that prevent death, sudden ascents and light-bodied flight, far-reaching inverted-sun travel, viewing hanging parks, drifting to Penglai, cultivating five virtues with morning sowing and evening harvest, sharing endlessness with rock, yellow-smelting transformations, turning hard ice to sludge, and color-changing five granaries - these are all deceitful men who confuse the masses, wield heterodox arts, and harbor fraud to deceive rulers.
translations.idiomatic: All who depart from the right way of benevolence and righteousness, ignore the normative words of the Five Classics, and loudly proclaim strange ghosts and spirits, vastly promoting ritual techniques, seeking reward from unblessed shrines, and saying there are immortals, life-ending medicines that prevent death, sudden ascents and light-bodied flight, far-reaching inverted-sun travel, viewing hanging parks, drifting to Penglai, cultivating five virtues with morning sowing and evening harvest, sharing endlessness with rock, yellow-smelting transformations, turning hard ice to sludge, and color-changing five granaries - these are all deceitful men who confuse the masses, wield heterodox arts, and harbor fraud to deceive rulers.

sentences.zh: 聽其言，洋洋滿耳，若將可遇；

translations.literal: Literal: Listening to them, their words overflow the ears as if they can be encountered.
translations.idiomatic: Listening to them, their words overflow the ears as if they can be encountered.

sentences.zh: 求之，盪盪如係風捕景，終不可得。

translations.literal: Literal: Seeking them, it is as empty as tying wind and catching shadows - never obtainable.
translations.idiomatic: Seeking them, it is as empty as tying wind and catching shadows - never obtainable.

sentences.zh: 是以明王距而不聽，聖人絕而不語。

translations.literal: Literal: Therefore enlightened kings reject and do not hear them; sages cut them off and do not speak of them.
translations.idiomatic: Therefore enlightened kings reject and do not hear them; sages cut them off and do not speak of them.

sentences.zh: 昔周史萇弘欲以鬼神之術輔尊靈王會朝諸侯，而周室愈微，諸侯愈叛。

translations.literal: Literal: Formerly Zhou historian Chang Hong tried to use spirit arts to assist King Ling's prestige in convening feudal audiences, yet Zhou became weaker and lords more rebellious.
translations.idiomatic: Formerly Zhou historian Chang Hong tried to use spirit arts to assist King Ling's prestige in convening feudal audiences, yet Zhou became weaker and lords more rebellious.

sentences.zh: 楚懷王隆祭祀，事鬼神，欲以獲福助，卻秦師，而兵挫地削，身辱國危。

translations.literal: Literal: King Huai of Chu exalted sacrifice and served ghosts and spirits, hoping for blessing to repel Qin armies, yet his forces were broken, land cut, person disgraced, and state imperiled.
translations.idiomatic: King Huai of Chu exalted sacrifice and served ghosts and spirits, hoping for blessing to repel Qin armies, yet his forces were broken, land cut, person disgraced, and state imperiled.

sentences.zh: 秦始皇初并天下，甘心於神僊之道，遣徐福、韓終之屬多齎童男童女入海求神采藥，因逃不還，天下怨恨。

translations.literal: Literal: When Qin First Emperor first unified the realm, he was devoted to immortal arts; he sent Xu Fu, Han Zhong, and others, heavily provisioned with boys and girls to sea seeking gods and medicines; they escaped and never returned, and the realm resented it.
translations.idiomatic: When Qin First Emperor first unified the realm, he was devoted to immortal arts; he sent Xu Fu, Han Zhong, and others, heavily provisioned with boys and girls to sea seeking gods and medicines; they escaped and never returned, and the realm resented it.

sentences.zh: 漢興，新垣平、齊人少翁、公孫卿、欒大等，皆以僊人黃冶祭祠事鬼使物入海求神采藥貴幸，賞賜累千金。

translations.literal: Literal: After Han arose, Xinyuan Ping, the Qi man Shaoweng, Gongsun Qing, Luan Da, and others were favored and rewarded with thousands of gold for immortal-smelting rites, ghost service, spirit manipulation, and sea expeditions for divine medicines.
translations.idiomatic: After Han arose, Xinyuan Ping, the Qi man Shaoweng, Gongsun Qing, Luan Da, and others were favored and rewarded with thousands of gold for immortal-smelting rites, ghost service, spirit manipulation, and sea expeditions for divine medicines.

sentences.zh: 大尤尊盛，至妻公主，爵位重絫，震動海內。

translations.literal: Literal: Luan Da was especially exalted, even marrying a princess, with ranks and offices stacked heavily and his prestige shaking the realm.
translations.idiomatic: Luan Da was especially exalted, even marrying a princess, with ranks and offices stacked heavily and his prestige shaking the realm.

sentences.zh: 元鼎、元封之際，燕齊之間方士瞋目扼掔，言有神僊祭祀致福之術者以萬數。

translations.literal: Literal: Between Yuanding and Yuanfeng, among Yan and Qi, fangshi glared and clenched fists; those claiming arts of divine sacrifice and blessing were numbered in the tens of thousands.
translations.idiomatic: Between Yuanding and Yuanfeng, among Yan and Qi, fangshi glared and clenched fists; those claiming arts of divine sacrifice and blessing were numbered in the tens of thousands.

sentences.zh: 其後，平等皆以術窮詐得，誅夷伏辜。

translations.literal: Literal: Later Ping and the others were all exposed as exhausted techniques and fraud, executed with clans annihilated.
translations.idiomatic: Later Ping and the others were all exposed as exhausted techniques and fraud, executed with clans annihilated.

sentences.zh: 至初元中，有天淵玉女、鉅鹿神人、轑陽侯師張宗之姦，紛紛復起。

translations.literal: Literal: By Chuyuan period there were again frauds such as Heavenly Abyss Jade Maiden, Julu Spirit-Man, and Zhang Zong, retainer of Lord Liaoyang, rising in confusion.
translations.idiomatic: By Chuyuan period there were again frauds such as Heavenly Abyss Jade Maiden, Julu Spirit-Man, and Zhang Zong, retainer of Lord Liaoyang, rising in confusion.

sentences.zh: 夫周秦之末，三五之隆，已嘗專意散財，厚爵祿，竦精神，舉天下以求之矣。

translations.literal: Literal: At the close of Zhou and Qin and even in times of great flourishing, rulers had already devoted full intent, scattered wealth, granted rich titles, roused spirits, and mobilized the realm to seek these things.
translations.idiomatic: At the close of Zhou and Qin and even in times of great flourishing, rulers had already devoted full intent, scattered wealth, granted rich titles, roused spirits, and mobilized the realm to seek these things.

sentences.zh: 曠日經年，靡有毫氂之驗，足以揆今。

translations.literal: Literal: After long years and decades, not even the slightest proof appeared - enough to judge the present.
translations.idiomatic: After long years and decades, not even the slightest proof appeared - enough to judge the present.

sentences.zh: 經曰：『享多儀，儀不及物，惟曰不享。』

translations.literal: Literal: The Classic says: 'Though many rites are performed, if rites do not reach the object, it is simply "not offered."'
translations.idiomatic: The Classic says: 'Though many rites are performed, if rites do not reach the object, it is simply "not offered."'

sentences.zh: 論語說曰：『子不語怪神。』

translations.literal: Literal: The Analects says: 'The Master did not speak of prodigies and spirits.'
translations.idiomatic: The Analects says: 'The Master did not speak of prodigies and spirits.'

sentences.zh: 唯陛下距絕此類，毋令姦人有以窺朝者。」

translations.literal: Literal: May Your Majesty reject this whole class and not let deceitful men find openings into court."
translations.idiomatic: May Your Majesty reject this whole class and not let deceitful men find openings into court."

sentences.zh: 上善其言。

translations.literal: Literal: The emperor approved his words.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor approved his words.

sentences.zh: 後成都侯王商為大司馬衛將軍輔政，杜鄴說商曰：「『東鄰殺牛，不如西鄰之瀹祭』，言奉天之道，貴以誠質大得民心也。

translations.literal: Literal: Later Chengdu Marquis Wang Shang, serving as Grand Marshal and Guard General in regency, was advised by Du Ye: "'The eastern neighbor's slaughtered ox is not as good as the western neighbor's simple boiled offering' - meaning in serving Heaven, sincerity and plainness best win the people's hearts.
translations.idiomatic: Later Chengdu Marquis Wang Shang, serving as Grand Marshal and Guard General in regency, was advised by Du Ye: "'The eastern neighbor's slaughtered ox is not as good as the western neighbor's simple boiled offering' - meaning in serving Heaven, sincerity and plainness best win the people's hearts.

sentences.zh: 行穢祀豐，猶不蒙祐；

translations.literal: Literal: Lavish but polluted sacrifice still gains no blessing.
translations.idiomatic: Lavish but polluted sacrifice still gains no blessing.

sentences.zh: 德修薦薄，吉必大來。

translations.literal: Literal: When virtue is cultivated though offerings are thin, great fortune must come.
translations.idiomatic: When virtue is cultivated though offerings are thin, great fortune must come.

sentences.zh: 古者壇場有常處，抠禋有常用，贊見有常禮；

translations.literal: Literal: In antiquity altar fields had fixed locations, purification rites fixed forms, and presentation rites fixed ceremony.
translations.idiomatic: In antiquity altar fields had fixed locations, purification rites fixed forms, and presentation rites fixed ceremony.

sentences.zh: 犧牲玉帛雖備而財不匱，車輿臣役雖動而用不勞。

translations.literal: Literal: Though victims, jade, and silk were complete, resources were not exhausted; though chariots, attendants, and labor moved, use was not burdensome.
translations.idiomatic: Though victims, jade, and silk were complete, resources were not exhausted; though chariots, attendants, and labor moved, use was not burdensome.

sentences.zh: 是故每奉其禮，助者歡說，大路所歷，黎元不知。

translations.literal: Literal: Therefore whenever rites were performed, assistants were joyful, and where great roads passed, common people scarcely noticed.
translations.idiomatic: Therefore whenever rites were performed, assistants were joyful, and where great roads passed, common people scarcely noticed.

sentences.zh: 今甘泉、河東天地郊祀，咸失方位，違陰陽之宜。

translations.literal: Literal: Now Ganquan and Hedong Heaven-Earth suburban rites both miss proper orientation, violating yin-yang fitness.
translations.idiomatic: Now Ganquan and Hedong Heaven-Earth suburban rites both miss proper orientation, violating yin-yang fitness.

sentences.zh: 及雍五畤皆曠遠，奉尊之役休而復起，繕治共張無解已時，皇天著象殆可略知。

translations.literal: Literal: And Yong's five altars are all remote; duties of honoring them cease and rise again, with endless repair and provisioning burdens; Heaven's omens have already made this roughly knowable.
translations.idiomatic: And Yong's five altars are all remote; duties of honoring them cease and rise again, with endless repair and provisioning burdens; Heaven's omens have already made this roughly knowable.

sentences.zh: 前上甘泉，先敺失道；

translations.literal: Literal: On the earlier visit to Ganquan, advance drivers lost the route.
translations.idiomatic: On the earlier visit to Ganquan, advance drivers lost the route.

sentences.zh: 禮月之夕，奉引復迷。

translations.literal: Literal: On the evening moon rite, the ceremonial leading party got lost again.
translations.idiomatic: On the evening moon rite, the ceremonial leading party got lost again.

sentences.zh: 祠后土還，臨河當渡，疾風起波，船不可御。

translations.literal: Literal: Returning from Houtu sacrifice, at river crossing strong winds raised waves and boats could not be controlled.
translations.idiomatic: Returning from Houtu sacrifice, at river crossing strong winds raised waves and boats could not be controlled.

sentences.zh: 又雍大雨，壞平陽宮垣。

translations.literal: Literal: Then at Yong heavy rain damaged the walls of Pingyang Palace.
translations.idiomatic: Then at Yong heavy rain damaged the walls of Pingyang Palace.

sentences.zh: 乃三月甲子，震電災林光宮門。

translations.literal: Literal: Then on third month jiazi, thunder and lightning disaster struck Lingguang Palace gate.
translations.idiomatic: Then on third month jiazi, thunder and lightning disaster struck Lingguang Palace gate.

sentences.zh: 祥瑞未著，咎徵仍臻。

translations.literal: Literal: Auspicious signs are not manifest, while ominous portents keep arriving.
translations.idiomatic: Auspicious signs are not manifest, while ominous portents keep arriving.

sentences.zh: 跡三郡所奏，皆有變故。

translations.literal: Literal: Looking at what three commanderies reported, all had abnormal incidents.
translations.idiomatic: Looking at what three commanderies reported, all had abnormal incidents.

sentences.zh: 不答不饗，何以甚此！

translations.literal: Literal: If there is no response and no acceptance, why is it this severe!
translations.idiomatic: If there is no response and no acceptance, why is it this severe!

sentences.zh: 《詩》曰『率由舊章』。

translations.literal: Literal: The Odes says, 'Follow the old standards.'
translations.idiomatic: The Odes says, 'Follow the old standards.'

sentences.zh: 舊章，先王法度，文王以之，交神于祀，子孫千億。

translations.literal: Literal: Old standards are laws of former kings; King Wen used them to commune with spirits through sacrifice, and his descendants reached into the hundreds of billions.
translations.idiomatic: Old standards are laws of former kings; King Wen used them to commune with spirits through sacrifice, and his descendants reached into the hundreds of billions.

sentences.zh: 宜如異時公卿之議，復還長安南北郊。」

translations.literal: Literal: It is proper to follow earlier ministerial deliberations and restore Chang'an southern and northern suburbs."
translations.idiomatic: It is proper to follow earlier ministerial deliberations and restore Chang'an southern and northern suburbs."

sentences.zh: 後數年，成帝崩，皇太后詔有司曰：「皇帝即位，思順天心，遵經義，定郊禮，天下說憙。

translations.literal: Literal: Some years later Emperor Cheng died; the Empress Dowager ordered officials: "When the emperor took the throne, he sought to follow Heaven's will, obey canonical meaning, and fix suburban rites, and the realm rejoiced.
translations.idiomatic: Some years later Emperor Cheng died; the Empress Dowager ordered officials: "When the emperor took the throne, he sought to follow Heaven's will, obey canonical meaning, and fix suburban rites, and the realm rejoiced.

sentences.zh: 懼未有皇孫，故復甘泉泰畤、汾陰后土，庶幾獲福。

translations.literal: Literal: Fearing no imperial grandson, he therefore restored Ganquan Tai altar and Fenyin Houtu, hoping to receive blessing.
translations.idiomatic: Fearing no imperial grandson, he therefore restored Ganquan Tai altar and Fenyin Houtu, hoping to receive blessing.

sentences.zh: 皇帝恨難之，卒未得其祐。

translations.literal: Literal: The emperor lamented the difficulty and in the end did not obtain that blessing.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor lamented the difficulty and in the end did not obtain that blessing.

sentences.zh: 其復南北郊長安如故，以順皇帝之意也。」

translations.literal: Literal: Restore southern and northern suburbs at Chang'an as before, to accord with the late emperor's intent."
translations.idiomatic: Restore southern and northern suburbs at Chang'an as before, to accord with the late emperor's intent."

sentences.zh: 哀帝即位，寢疾，博徵方術士，京師諸縣皆有侍祠使者，盡復前世所常興諸神祠官，凡七百餘所，一歲三萬七千祠云。

translations.literal: Literal: When Emperor Ai took the throne and became chronically ill, he widely summoned technical specialists. In capital and counties there were ritual envoys everywhere, and all the commonly revived divine shrines and offices of previous ages were restored, over seven hundred sites, said to total 37,000 rites yearly.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Ai took the throne and became chronically ill, he widely summoned technical specialists. In capital and counties there were ritual envoys everywhere, and all the commonly revived divine shrines and offices of previous ages were restored, over seven hundred sites, said to total 37,000 rites yearly.

sentences.zh: 明年，復令太皇太后詔有司曰：「皇帝孝順，奉承聖業，靡有解怠，而久疾未瘳。

translations.literal: Literal: The next year, the Grand Empress Dowager again ordered officials: "The emperor is filial and compliant, carries on sacred enterprise without slackness, yet his long illness is unhealed.
translations.idiomatic: The next year, the Grand Empress Dowager again ordered officials: "The emperor is filial and compliant, carries on sacred enterprise without slackness, yet his long illness is unhealed.

sentences.zh: 夙夜唯思，殆繼體之君不宜改作。

translations.literal: Literal: Day and night I think only this: perhaps an heir-ruler should not alter established institutions.
translations.idiomatic: Day and night I think only this: perhaps an heir-ruler should not alter established institutions.

sentences.zh: 其復甘泉泰畤、汾陰后土祠如故。」

translations.literal: Literal: Restore Ganquan Tai altar and Fenyin Houtu rites as before."
translations.idiomatic: Restore Ganquan Tai altar and Fenyin Houtu rites as before."

sentences.zh: 上亦不能親至，遣有司行事而禮祠焉。

translations.literal: Literal: The emperor could not personally go, and sent officials to perform the rites.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor could not personally go, and sent officials to perform the rites.

sentences.zh: 後三年，哀帝崩。

translations.literal: Literal: Three years later, Emperor Ai died.
translations.idiomatic: Three years later, Emperor Ai died.

sentences.zh: 平帝元始五年，大司馬王莽奏言：「王者父事天，故爵稱天子。

translations.literal: Literal: In fifth year Yuanshi of Emperor Ping, Grand Marshal Wang Mang memorialized: "A ruler serves Heaven as a son serves father, therefore his title is Son of Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: In fifth year Yuanshi of Emperor Ping, Grand Marshal Wang Mang memorialized: "A ruler serves Heaven as a son serves father, therefore his title is Son of Heaven.

sentences.zh: 孔子曰：『人之行莫大於孝，孝莫大於嚴父，嚴父莫大於配天。』

translations.literal: Literal: Confucius said: 'In human conduct nothing is greater than filiality; in filiality nothing is greater than honoring one's father; in honoring one's father nothing is greater than pairing him with Heaven.'
translations.idiomatic: Confucius said: 'In human conduct nothing is greater than filiality; in filiality nothing is greater than honoring one's father; in honoring one's father nothing is greater than pairing him with Heaven.'

sentences.zh: 王者尊其考，欲以配天，緣考之意，欲尊祖，推而上之，遂及始祖。

translations.literal: Literal: A ruler honors his father and wishes to pair him with Heaven; following this intention he wishes to honor ancestors, and by extension reaches up to the first ancestor.
translations.idiomatic: A ruler honors his father and wishes to pair him with Heaven; following this intention he wishes to honor ancestors, and by extension reaches up to the first ancestor.

sentences.zh: 是以周公郊祀后稷以配天，宗祀文王於明堂以配上帝。

translations.literal: Literal: Thus the Duke of Zhou sacrificed to Houji in the suburbs to pair with Heaven, and in the Mingtang gave ancestral sacrifice to King Wen to pair with the High Thearch.
translations.idiomatic: Thus the Duke of Zhou sacrificed to Houji in the suburbs to pair with Heaven, and in the Mingtang gave ancestral sacrifice to King Wen to pair with the High Thearch.

sentences.zh: 禮記天子祭天地及山川，歲遍。

translations.literal: Literal: The Liji says the Son of Heaven sacrifices to Heaven and Earth and to mountains and rivers, covering all through the year.
translations.idiomatic: The Liji says the Son of Heaven sacrifices to Heaven and Earth and to mountains and rivers, covering all through the year.

sentences.zh: 春秋穀梁傳以十二月下辛卜，正月上辛郊。

translations.literal: Literal: The Guliang Commentary says one divines on lower-xin day of twelfth month and performs suburban sacrifice on upper-xin day of first month.
translations.idiomatic: The Guliang Commentary says one divines on lower-xin day of twelfth month and performs suburban sacrifice on upper-xin day of first month.

sentences.zh: 高皇帝受命，因雍四畤起北畤，而備五帝，未共王地之祀。

translations.literal: Literal: Gao Emperor, after receiving mandate, based himself on Yong's four altars and set up Northern altar, completing the five Thearchs, but did not yet include the land's own rites.
translations.idiomatic: Gao Emperor, after receiving mandate, based himself on Yong's four altars and set up Northern altar, completing the five Thearchs, but did not yet include the land's own rites.

sentences.zh: 孝文十六年用新垣平，初起渭陽五帝廟，祭泰一、地祇，以太祖高皇帝配。

translations.literal: Literal: In Emperor Wen's sixteenth year, by using Xinyuan Ping, he first established Weiyang Five-Thearch temple, sacrificing to Taiyi and earthly powers, with Gao Emperor as paired ancestor.
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Wen's sixteenth year, by using Xinyuan Ping, he first established Weiyang Five-Thearch temple, sacrificing to Taiyi and earthly powers, with Gao Emperor as paired ancestor.

sentences.zh: 日冬至祠泰一，夏至祠地祇，皆并祠五帝，而共一牲，上親郊拜。

translations.literal: Literal: At winter solstice he sacrificed to Taiyi, at summer solstice to earthly powers, both together with five Thearchs, sharing one victim, with the emperor personally attending suburban bows.
translations.idiomatic: At winter solstice he sacrificed to Taiyi, at summer solstice to earthly powers, both together with five Thearchs, sharing one victim, with the emperor personally attending suburban bows.

sentences.zh: 後平伏誅，乃不復自親，而使有司行事。

translations.literal: Literal: After Ping was executed, the emperor no longer attended personally and had officials perform the rites.
translations.idiomatic: After Ping was executed, the emperor no longer attended personally and had officials perform the rites.

sentences.zh: 孝武皇帝祠雍，曰：『今上帝朕親郊，而后土無祠，則禮不答也。』

translations.literal: Literal: When Emperor Wu sacrificed at Yong, he said: 'Now I personally make suburban rite to the High Thearch, yet Houtu has no sacrifice - then ritual reciprocity is lacking.'"
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Wu sacrificed at Yong, he said: 'Now I personally make suburban rite to the High Thearch, yet Houtu has no sacrifice - then ritual reciprocity is lacking.'"

sentences.zh: 於是元鼎四年十一月甲子始立后土祠於汾陰。

translations.literal: Literal: Thus in yuanding fourth year, on jiazi day of the eleventh month, the Houtu shrine was first established at Fenyin.
translations.idiomatic: Thus in yuanding fourth year, on jiazi day of the eleventh month, the Houtu shrine was first established at Fenyin.

sentences.zh: 或曰，五帝，泰一之佐，宜立泰一。

translations.literal: Literal: Some said: "The Five Thearchs are attendants of Taiyi; Taiyi should be established."
translations.idiomatic: Some said: "The Five Thearchs are attendants of Taiyi; Taiyi should be established."

sentences.zh: 五年十一月癸未始立泰一祠於甘泉，二歲一郊，與雍更祠，亦以高祖配，不歲事天，皆未應古制。

translations.literal: Literal: So in fifth year, on guiwei day of the eleventh month, Taiyi shrine was first established at Ganquan; suburban rite every two years, alternating with Yong rites, with Gaozu as paired ancestor, and without annual Heaven service - all still not matching ancient institutions.
translations.idiomatic: So in fifth year, on guiwei day of the eleventh month, Taiyi shrine was first established at Ganquan; suburban rite every two years, alternating with Yong rites, with Gaozu as paired ancestor, and without annual Heaven service - all still not matching ancient institutions.

sentences.zh: 建始元年，徙甘泉泰畤、河東后土於長安南北郊。

translations.literal: Literal: In jianshi first year, Ganquan Tai altar and Hedong Houtu were moved to southern and northern suburbs of Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: In jianshi first year, Ganquan Tai altar and Hedong Houtu were moved to southern and northern suburbs of Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 永始元年三月，以未有皇孫，復甘泉、河東祠。

translations.literal: Literal: In yongshi first year, third month, because there was still no imperial grandson, Ganquan and Hedong rites were restored.
translations.idiomatic: In yongshi first year, third month, because there was still no imperial grandson, Ganquan and Hedong rites were restored.

sentences.zh: 綏和二年，以卒不獲祐，復長安南北郊。

translations.literal: Literal: In suihe second year, since blessing still was not obtained, they restored southern and northern suburbs of Chang'an again.
translations.idiomatic: In suihe second year, since blessing still was not obtained, they restored southern and northern suburbs of Chang'an again.

sentences.zh: 建平三年，懼孝哀皇帝之疾未瘳，復甘泉、汾陰祠，竟復無福。

translations.literal: Literal: In jianping third year, fearing Emperor Ai's illness remained unhealed, Ganquan and Fenyin rites were restored, yet in the end still no blessing came.
translations.idiomatic: In jianping third year, fearing Emperor Ai's illness remained unhealed, Ganquan and Fenyin rites were restored, yet in the end still no blessing came.

sentences.zh: 臣謹與太師孔光、長樂少府平晏、大司農左咸、中壘校尉劉歆、太中大夫朱陽、博士薛順、議郎國由等六十七人議，皆曰宜如建始時丞相衡等議，復長安南北郊如故。」

translations.literal: Literal: I have carefully deliberated with Grand Tutor Kong Guang, Changle Lesser Treasury Ping Yan, Grand Minister of Agriculture Zuo Xian, Inner Rampart Colonel Liu Xin, Grandee Zhu Yang, Erudite Xue Shun, Consulting Gentleman Guo You and others, sixty-seven in all; all said it should follow the jianshi-era deliberation of Chancellor Heng and others and restore Chang'an south and north suburbs as before."
translations.idiomatic: I have carefully deliberated with Grand Tutor Kong Guang, Changle Lesser Treasury Ping Yan, Grand Minister of Agriculture Zuo Xian, Inner Rampart Colonel Liu Xin, Grandee Zhu Yang, Erudite Xue Shun, Consulting Gentleman Guo You and others, sixty-seven in all; all said it should follow the jianshi-era deliberation of Chancellor Heng and others and restore Chang'an south and north suburbs as before."

sentences.zh: 莽又頗改其祭禮，曰：「周官天墬之祀，樂有別有合。

translations.literal: Literal: Wang Mang further altered sacrificial ritual somewhat, saying: "In Zhou offices, rites to Heaven and Earth include separate and combined music.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Mang further altered sacrificial ritual somewhat, saying: "In Zhou offices, rites to Heaven and Earth include separate and combined music.

sentences.zh: 其合樂曰『以六律、六鐘、五聲、八音、六舞大合樂』，祀天神，祭墬祇，祀四望，祭山川，享先妣先祖。

translations.literal: Literal: The combined music says: 'Using six pitch-pipes, six bells, five tones, eight instruments, and six dances in great combined music' to sacrifice heavenly gods, earthly powers, the four distant quarters, mountains and rivers, and to enjoy former mothers and former ancestors.
translations.idiomatic: The combined music says: 'Using six pitch-pipes, six bells, five tones, eight instruments, and six dances in great combined music' to sacrifice heavenly gods, earthly powers, the four distant quarters, mountains and rivers, and to enjoy former mothers and former ancestors.

sentences.zh: 凡六樂，奏六歌，而天墬神祇之物皆至。

translations.literal: Literal: In all six musics, six songs are played, and all offerings for Heaven, Earth, gods, and powers arrive.
translations.idiomatic: In all six musics, six songs are played, and all offerings for Heaven, Earth, gods, and powers arrive.

sentences.zh: 四望，蓋謂日月星海也。

translations.literal: Literal: The four distant quarters likely mean sun, moon, stars, and sea.
translations.idiomatic: The four distant quarters likely mean sun, moon, stars, and sea.

sentences.zh: 三光高而不可得親，海廣大無限界，故其樂同。

translations.literal: Literal: The three lights are high and cannot be approached closely; the sea is vast and boundless, so their music is the same.
translations.idiomatic: The three lights are high and cannot be approached closely; the sea is vast and boundless, so their music is the same.

sentences.zh: 祀天則天文從。

translations.literal: Literal: When sacrificing Heaven, heavenly patterns are followed.
translations.idiomatic: When sacrificing Heaven, heavenly patterns are followed.

sentences.zh: 祭墬則墬理從。

translations.literal: Literal: When sacrificing Earth, earthly principles are followed.
translations.idiomatic: When sacrificing Earth, earthly principles are followed.

sentences.zh: 三光，天文也。

translations.literal: Literal: The three lights belong to heavenly pattern.
translations.idiomatic: The three lights belong to heavenly pattern.

sentences.zh: 山川，地理也。

translations.literal: Literal: Mountains and rivers belong to earthly pattern.
translations.idiomatic: Mountains and rivers belong to earthly pattern.

sentences.zh: 天地合祭，先祖配天，先妣配墬，其誼一也。

translations.literal: Literal: Combined sacrifice to Heaven and Earth, with first ancestor paired to Heaven and first ancestress paired to Earth, has one underlying principle.
translations.idiomatic: Combined sacrifice to Heaven and Earth, with first ancestor paired to Heaven and first ancestress paired to Earth, has one underlying principle.

sentences.zh: 天墬合精，夫婦判合。

translations.literal: Literal: Heaven and Earth join essences, as husband and wife separate and unite.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven and Earth join essences, as husband and wife separate and unite.

sentences.zh: 祭天南郊，則以墬配，一體之誼也。

translations.literal: Literal: When sacrificing Heaven at southern suburb, Earth is paired with it - principle of one body.
translations.idiomatic: When sacrificing Heaven at southern suburb, Earth is paired with it - principle of one body.

sentences.zh: 天墬位皆南鄉，同席，墬在東，共牢而食。

translations.literal: Literal: Heaven and Earth seats both face south on shared mat; Earth is in the east, sharing one victim and meal.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven and Earth seats both face south on shared mat; Earth is in the east, sharing one victim and meal.

sentences.zh: 高帝、高后配於壇上，西鄉，后在北，亦同席共牢。

translations.literal: Literal: Gao Emperor and Gao Empress are paired above altar, facing west; Empress is in north, also sharing one mat and victim.
translations.idiomatic: Gao Emperor and Gao Empress are paired above altar, facing west; Empress is in north, also sharing one mat and victim.

sentences.zh: 牲用繭栗，玄酒陶匏。

translations.literal: Literal: Victims use chestnut-brown calves; dark ritual wine and pottery-gourd vessels.
translations.idiomatic: Victims use chestnut-brown calves; dark ritual wine and pottery-gourd vessels.

sentences.zh: 禮記曰天子籍田千具以事天墬，繇是言之，宜有黍稷。

translations.literal: Literal: The Liji says the Son of Heaven tills a thousand plots to serve Heaven and Earth; from this, millet and grain should be used.
translations.idiomatic: The Liji says the Son of Heaven tills a thousand plots to serve Heaven and Earth; from this, millet and grain should be used.

sentences.zh: 天地用牲一，燔抠瘞薶用牲一，高帝、高后用牲一。

translations.literal: Literal: One victim is used for Heaven-Earth, one for burned and buried rites, and one for Gao Emperor and Gao Empress.
translations.idiomatic: One victim is used for Heaven-Earth, one for burned and buried rites, and one for Gao Emperor and Gao Empress.

sentences.zh: 天用牲左，及黍稷燔抠南郊；

translations.literal: Literal: Heaven uses left-side victim, with millet and grain burned at southern suburb.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven uses left-side victim, with millet and grain burned at southern suburb.

sentences.zh: 墬用牲右，及黍稷瘞於北郊。

translations.literal: Literal: Earth uses right-side victim, with millet and grain buried at northern suburb.
translations.idiomatic: Earth uses right-side victim, with millet and grain buried at northern suburb.

sentences.zh: 其旦，東鄉再拜朝日；

translations.literal: Literal: At dawn, facing east, one bows twice to greet the sun.
translations.idiomatic: At dawn, facing east, one bows twice to greet the sun.

sentences.zh: 其夕，西鄉再拜夕月。

translations.literal: Literal: At dusk, facing west, one bows twice to greet the moon.
translations.idiomatic: At dusk, facing west, one bows twice to greet the moon.

sentences.zh: 然後孝弟之道備，而神衹嘉享，萬福降輯。

translations.literal: Literal: Then the way of filiality and fraternality is complete, divine powers gladly receive offerings, and ten thousand blessings gather.
translations.idiomatic: Then the way of filiality and fraternality is complete, divine powers gladly receive offerings, and ten thousand blessings gather.

sentences.zh: 此天墬合祀，以祖妣配者也。

translations.literal: Literal: This is combined sacrifice to Heaven and Earth with ancestor and ancestress as pairings.
translations.idiomatic: This is combined sacrifice to Heaven and Earth with ancestor and ancestress as pairings.

sentences.zh: 其別樂曰『冬日至，於墬上之圜丘奏樂六變，則天神皆降；

translations.literal: Literal: The separate music says: 'At winter solstice, on the round mound atop Earth, play music with six changes and heavenly gods all descend;
translations.idiomatic: The separate music says: 'At winter solstice, on the round mound atop Earth, play music with six changes and heavenly gods all descend;

sentences.zh: 夏日至，於澤中之方丘奏樂八變，則墬衹皆出。』

translations.literal: Literal: at summer solstice, on square mound in marshes, play music with eight changes and earthly powers all emerge.'
translations.idiomatic: at summer solstice, on square mound in marshes, play music with eight changes and earthly powers all emerge.'

sentences.zh: 天墬有常位，不得常合，此其各特祀者也。

translations.literal: Literal: Heaven and Earth have fixed positions and cannot always be combined; these are their separate rites.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven and Earth have fixed positions and cannot always be combined; these are their separate rites.

sentences.zh: 陰陽之別於日冬夏至，其會也以孟春正月上辛若丁。

translations.literal: Literal: Yin and yang divide at winter and summer solstice days; they join on first month upper-xin or ding days of early spring.
translations.idiomatic: Yin and yang divide at winter and summer solstice days; they join on first month upper-xin or ding days of early spring.

sentences.zh: 天子親合祀天墬於南郊，以高帝、高后配。

translations.literal: Literal: The Son of Heaven personally combines Heaven-Earth sacrifice at southern suburb, with Gao Emperor and Gao Empress as pairings.
translations.idiomatic: The Son of Heaven personally combines Heaven-Earth sacrifice at southern suburb, with Gao Emperor and Gao Empress as pairings.

sentences.zh: 陰陽有離合，《易》曰『分陰分陽，迭用柔剛』。

translations.literal: Literal: Yin and yang have separation and union; the Changes says, 'Divide yin and divide yang, alternately employing soft and hard.'
translations.idiomatic: Yin and yang have separation and union; the Changes says, 'Divide yin and divide yang, alternately employing soft and hard.'

sentences.zh: 以日冬至使有司奉祠南郊，高帝配而望群陽，日夏至使有司奉祭北郊，高后配而望群陰，皆以助致微氣，通道幽弱。

translations.literal: Literal: On winter solstice, officials perform southern suburb rite with Gao Emperor paired, viewing all yang; on summer solstice, officials perform northern suburb rite with Gao Empress paired, viewing all yin - both helping to bring subtle qi and connect to hidden weakness.
translations.idiomatic: On winter solstice, officials perform southern suburb rite with Gao Emperor paired, viewing all yang; on summer solstice, officials perform northern suburb rite with Gao Empress paired, viewing all yin - both helping to bring subtle qi and connect to hidden weakness.

sentences.zh: 當此之時，后不省方，故天子不親而遣有司，所以正承天順地，復聖王之制，顯太祖之功也。

translations.literal: Literal: At these times the empress does not go on inspection tours; thus the Son of Heaven does not go personally but dispatches officials, to correctly receive Heaven and follow Earth, restore sage-king institutions, and manifest the great ancestor's merit.
translations.idiomatic: At these times the empress does not go on inspection tours; thus the Son of Heaven does not go personally but dispatches officials, to correctly receive Heaven and follow Earth, restore sage-king institutions, and manifest the great ancestor's merit.

sentences.zh: 渭陽祠勿復修。

translations.literal: Literal: The Weiyang shrine should no longer be repaired.
translations.idiomatic: The Weiyang shrine should no longer be repaired.

sentences.zh: 群望未定悉定，定復奏。」

translations.literal: Literal: All unsettled group-offering sites should be settled and then memorialized again."
translations.idiomatic: All unsettled group-offering sites should be settled and then memorialized again."

sentences.zh: 奏可。

translations.literal: Literal: The memorial was approved.
translations.idiomatic: The memorial was approved.

sentences.zh: 三十餘年間，天地之祠五徙焉。

translations.literal: Literal: Within just over thirty years, Heaven-Earth rites moved five times.
translations.idiomatic: Within just over thirty years, Heaven-Earth rites moved five times.

sentences.zh: 後莽又奏言：「書曰『類於上帝，禋于六宗』。

translations.literal: Literal: Later Wang Mang further memorialized: "The Book says, 'Class sacrifice to the High Thearch; yin rite to six powers.'
translations.idiomatic: Later Wang Mang further memorialized: "The Book says, 'Class sacrifice to the High Thearch; yin rite to six powers.'

sentences.zh: 歐陽、大小夏侯三家說六宗，皆曰上不及天，下不及墬，旁不及四方，在六者之間，助陰陽變化，實一而名六，名實不相應。

translations.literal: Literal: In Ouyang and both Xiahou schools' explanations, the six powers are above not reaching Heaven, below not reaching Earth, and beside not reaching four directions; they are between the six, aiding yin-yang transformation; in reality one but named six, with name and substance mismatched.
translations.idiomatic: In Ouyang and both Xiahou schools' explanations, the six powers are above not reaching Heaven, below not reaching Earth, and beside not reaching four directions; they are between the six, aiding yin-yang transformation; in reality one but named six, with name and substance mismatched.

sentences.zh: 禮記祀典，功施於民則祀之。

translations.literal: Literal: In Liji sacrificial canons, those whose merit benefited people are sacrificed to.
translations.idiomatic: In Liji sacrificial canons, those whose merit benefited people are sacrificed to.

sentences.zh: 天文日月星辰，所昭仰也；

translations.literal: Literal: In heavenly pattern: sun, moon, stars.
translations.idiomatic: In heavenly pattern: sun, moon, stars.

sentences.zh: 地理山川海澤，所生殖也。

translations.literal: Literal: In earthly pattern: mountains, rivers, seas, marshes, where growth is generated.
translations.idiomatic: In earthly pattern: mountains, rivers, seas, marshes, where growth is generated.

sentences.zh: 易有八卦，乾坤六子，水火不相逮，雷風不相誖，山澤通氣，然後能變化，既成萬物也。

translations.literal: Literal: The Changes has eight trigrams: Qian-Kun and six children; water and fire do not overtake each other, thunder and wind do not oppose each other, mountain and marsh communicate qi, and only then transformation can occur and all things be completed.
translations.idiomatic: The Changes has eight trigrams: Qian-Kun and six children; water and fire do not overtake each other, thunder and wind do not oppose each other, mountain and marsh communicate qi, and only then transformation can occur and all things be completed.

sentences.zh: 臣前奏徙甘泉泰畤、汾陰后土皆復於南北郊。

translations.literal: Literal: I previously memorialized moving Ganquan Tai altar and Fenyin Houtu, both restored to south and north suburbs.
translations.idiomatic: I previously memorialized moving Ganquan Tai altar and Fenyin Houtu, both restored to south and north suburbs.

sentences.zh: 謹案周官『兆五帝於四郊』，山川各因其方，今五帝兆居在雍五畤，不合於古。

translations.literal: Literal: Carefully examining Zhou offices, 'The five Thearchs are marked in four suburbs'; mountains and rivers each follow their direction. Now five-Thearch markers remain at Yong five altars, not matching antiquity.
translations.idiomatic: Carefully examining Zhou offices, 'The five Thearchs are marked in four suburbs'; mountains and rivers each follow their direction. Now five-Thearch markers remain at Yong five altars, not matching antiquity.

sentences.zh: 又日月雷風山澤，易卦六子之尊氣，所謂六宗也。

translations.literal: Literal: Further, sun, moon, thunder, wind, mountain, and marsh are the esteemed qi of the six children in Changes trigrams - these are the so-called six powers.
translations.idiomatic: Further, sun, moon, thunder, wind, mountain, and marsh are the esteemed qi of the six children in Changes trigrams - these are the so-called six powers.

sentences.zh: 星辰水火溝瀆，皆六宗之屬也。

translations.literal: Literal: Stars, waters, fires, and channels all belong under six-powers category.
translations.idiomatic: Stars, waters, fires, and channels all belong under six-powers category.

sentences.zh: 今或未特祀，或無兆居。

translations.literal: Literal: Now some lack separate sacrifice, and some lack fixed marked seats.
translations.idiomatic: Now some lack separate sacrifice, and some lack fixed marked seats.

sentences.zh: 謹與太師光、大司徒宮、羲和歆等八十九人議，皆曰天子父事天，母事墬，今稱天神曰皇天上帝，泰一兆曰泰畤，而稱地祇曰后土，與中央黃靈同，又兆北郊未有尊稱。

translations.literal: Literal: I carefully deliberated with Grand Tutor Guang, Grand Minister of Works Gong, Xihe Liu Xin and others, eighty-nine in all; all said the Son of Heaven serves Heaven as father and Earth as mother. Now heavenly gods are called August Heaven High Thearch, and Taiyi marker called Tai altar, while earthly powers are called Houtu, same title as central Yellow Spirit, and northern suburb marker still has no honored title.
translations.idiomatic: I carefully deliberated with Grand Tutor Guang, Grand Minister of Works Gong, Xihe Liu Xin and others, eighty-nine in all; all said the Son of Heaven serves Heaven as father and Earth as mother. Now heavenly gods are called August Heaven High Thearch, and Taiyi marker called Tai altar, while earthly powers are called Houtu, same title as central Yellow Spirit, and northern suburb marker still has no honored title.

sentences.zh: 宜令地祇稱皇墬后祇，兆曰廣畤。

translations.literal: Literal: It should be ordered that Earth powers be titled August Earth Sovereign Power, with marker called Broad altar.
translations.idiomatic: It should be ordered that Earth powers be titled August Earth Sovereign Power, with marker called Broad altar.

sentences.zh: 《易》曰『方以類聚，物以群分』。

translations.literal: Literal: The Changes says, 'By kind, forms gather; by group, things divide.'
translations.idiomatic: The Changes says, 'By kind, forms gather; by group, things divide.'

sentences.zh: 分群神以類相從為五部，兆天墬之別神：中央帝黃靈后土畤及日廟、北辰、北斗、填星、中宿中宮於長安城之未墬兆；

translations.literal: Literal: Group all gods by category into five divisions and mark separate gods of Heaven and Earth: central Thearch Yellow Spirit and Houtu altar, plus sun temple, north pole, northern dipper, Saturn, central lodges and central palace at Chang'an's weidi marker;
translations.idiomatic: Group all gods by category into five divisions and mark separate gods of Heaven and Earth: central Thearch Yellow Spirit and Houtu altar, plus sun temple, north pole, northern dipper, Saturn, central lodges and central palace at Chang'an's weidi marker;

sentences.zh: 東方帝太昊青靈勾芒畤及雷公、風伯廟、歲星、東宿東宮於東郊兆；

translations.literal: Literal: eastern Thearch Taihao Azure Spirit Goumang altar, plus Thunder Duke, Wind Earl temples, Jupiter, eastern lodges and eastern palace at eastern suburb marker;
translations.idiomatic: eastern Thearch Taihao Azure Spirit Goumang altar, plus Thunder Duke, Wind Earl temples, Jupiter, eastern lodges and eastern palace at eastern suburb marker;

sentences.zh: 南方炎帝赤靈祝融畤及熒惑星、南宿南宮於南郊兆；

translations.literal: Literal: southern Flame Thearch Red Spirit Zhurong altar, plus Mars, southern lodges and southern palace at southern suburb marker;
translations.idiomatic: southern Flame Thearch Red Spirit Zhurong altar, plus Mars, southern lodges and southern palace at southern suburb marker;

sentences.zh: 西方帝少皞白靈蓐收畤及太白星、西宿西宮於西郊兆；

translations.literal: Literal: western Thearch Shaohao White Spirit Rushou altar, plus Venus, western lodges and western palace at western suburb marker;
translations.idiomatic: western Thearch Shaohao White Spirit Rushou altar, plus Venus, western lodges and western palace at western suburb marker;

sentences.zh: 北方帝顓頊黑靈玄冥畤及月廟、雨師廟、辰星、北宿北宮於北郊兆。」

translations.literal: Literal: northern Thearch Zhuanxu Black Spirit Xuanming altar, plus moon temple, Rain Master temple, Mercury, northern lodges and northern palace at northern suburb marker."
translations.idiomatic: northern Thearch Zhuanxu Black Spirit Xuanming altar, plus moon temple, Rain Master temple, Mercury, northern lodges and northern palace at northern suburb marker."

sentences.zh: 奏可。

translations.literal: Literal: The memorial was approved.
translations.idiomatic: The memorial was approved.

sentences.zh: 於是長安旁諸廟兆畤甚盛矣。

translations.literal: Literal: Thereupon shrines and marked altars around Chang'an became extremely numerous.
translations.idiomatic: Thereupon shrines and marked altars around Chang'an became extremely numerous.

sentences.zh: 莽又言：「帝王建立社稷，百王不易。

translations.literal: Literal: Wang Mang again said: "Kings establish altars of Soil and Grain; a hundred kings do not alter this.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Mang again said: "Kings establish altars of Soil and Grain; a hundred kings do not alter this.

sentences.zh: 社者，土也。

translations.literal: Literal: She means soil.
translations.idiomatic: She means soil.

sentences.zh: 宗廟，王者所居。

translations.literal: Literal: Ancestral temple is where kings dwell.
translations.idiomatic: Ancestral temple is where kings dwell.

sentences.zh: 稷者，百穀之王，所以奉宗廟，共粢盛，人所食以生活也。

translations.literal: Literal: Ji is king of hundred grains, serving ancestral temples with grain offerings and sustaining human life.
translations.idiomatic: Ji is king of hundred grains, serving ancestral temples with grain offerings and sustaining human life.

sentences.zh: 王者莫不尊重親祭，自為之主，禮如宗廟。

translations.literal: Literal: Rulers all honor and personally sacrifice, taking themselves as principal officiant, with ritual like ancestral temple.
translations.idiomatic: Rulers all honor and personally sacrifice, taking themselves as principal officiant, with ritual like ancestral temple.

sentences.zh: 《詩》曰『乃立冢土』。

translations.literal: Literal: The Odes says, 'Then he established the great earth altar.'
translations.idiomatic: The Odes says, 'Then he established the great earth altar.'

sentences.zh: 又曰『以御田祖，以祈甘雨』。

translations.literal: Literal: It also says, 'To direct the field-ancestor and pray for sweet rain.'
translations.idiomatic: It also says, 'To direct the field-ancestor and pray for sweet rain.'

sentences.zh: 《禮記》曰『唯祭宗廟社稷，為越紼而行事』。

translations.literal: Literal: The Liji says, 'Only for ancestral temple and Soil-Grain rites does one cross the funeral cords and proceed with the rite.'
translations.idiomatic: The Liji says, 'Only for ancestral temple and Soil-Grain rites does one cross the funeral cords and proceed with the rite.'

sentences.zh: 聖漢興，禮儀稍定，已有官社，未立官稷。」

translations.literal: Literal: When Sacred Han arose, rites gradually stabilized; there was already official Soil altar, but no official Grain altar."
translations.idiomatic: When Sacred Han arose, rites gradually stabilized; there was already official Soil altar, but no official Grain altar."

sentences.zh: 遂於官社後立官稷，以夏禹配食官社，后稷配食官稷。

translations.literal: Literal: So behind the official Soil altar he established official Grain altar, pairing Xia Yu in official Soil and Houji in official Grain offerings.
translations.idiomatic: So behind the official Soil altar he established official Grain altar, pairing Xia Yu in official Soil and Houji in official Grain offerings.

sentences.zh: 稷種穀樹。

translations.literal: Literal: At Grain altar he planted grain crops and trees.
translations.idiomatic: At Grain altar he planted grain crops and trees.

sentences.zh: 徐州牧歲貢五色土各一斗。

translations.literal: Literal: The Xuzhou governor annually presented one dou each of five-colored soils.
translations.idiomatic: The Xuzhou governor annually presented one dou each of five-colored soils.

sentences.zh: 莽篡位二年，興神僊事，以方士蘇樂言，起八風臺於宮中。

translations.literal: Literal: In second year after Wang Mang's usurpation, he promoted immortal affairs; on fangshi Su Le's advice, he built Eight Winds Terrace inside palace.
translations.idiomatic: In second year after Wang Mang's usurpation, he promoted immortal affairs; on fangshi Su Le's advice, he built Eight Winds Terrace inside palace.

sentences.zh: 臺成萬金，作樂其上，順風作液湯。

translations.literal: Literal: The terrace cost ten thousand gold; music was performed atop it, and wind-following liquid soup was prepared.
translations.idiomatic: The terrace cost ten thousand gold; music was performed atop it, and wind-following liquid soup was prepared.

sentences.zh: 又種五粱禾於殿中，各順色置其方面，先煮鶴齔、毒冒、犀玉二十餘物漬種，計粟斛成一金，言此黃帝穀僊之術也。

translations.literal: Literal: He also planted five kinds of grain in palace halls, placing each by color and direction; he first boiled over twenty substances including crane-bones, du-mao, rhinoceros jade, and soaked seeds in them, calculating one hu of millet became worth one gold, saying this was Yellow Thearch's immortal grain art.
translations.idiomatic: He also planted five kinds of grain in palace halls, placing each by color and direction; he first boiled over twenty substances including crane-bones, du-mao, rhinoceros jade, and soaked seeds in them, calculating one hu of millet became worth one gold, saying this was Yellow Thearch's immortal grain art.

sentences.zh: 以樂為黃門郎，令主之。

translations.literal: Literal: He made Le a Palace Gentleman and ordered him to manage it.
translations.idiomatic: He made Le a Palace Gentleman and ordered him to manage it.

sentences.zh: 莽遂崇鬼神淫祀，至其末年，自天地六宗以下至諸小鬼神，凡千七百所，用三牲鳥獸三千餘種。

translations.literal: Literal: Mang then exalted ghosts and licentious sacrifice; by his final years, from Heaven-Earth six powers down to minor spirits, there were 1,700 sites, using over 3,000 kinds of three-victim birds and beasts.
translations.idiomatic: Mang then exalted ghosts and licentious sacrifice; by his final years, from Heaven-Earth six powers down to minor spirits, there were 1,700 sites, using over 3,000 kinds of three-victim birds and beasts.

sentences.zh: 後不能備，乃以雞當鶩鴈，犬當麋鹿。

translations.literal: Literal: Later he could not provide all of them, and substituted chickens for ducks and geese, dogs for elk and deer.
translations.idiomatic: Later he could not provide all of them, and substituted chickens for ducks and geese, dogs for elk and deer.

sentences.zh: 數下詔自以當僊，語在其傳。

translations.literal: Literal: He repeatedly issued edicts claiming he should become immortal; details are in his biography.
translations.idiomatic: He repeatedly issued edicts claiming he should become immortal; details are in his biography.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：漢興之初，庶事草創，唯一叔孫生略定朝廷之儀。

translations.literal: Literal: Appraisal says: At the beginning of Han, all affairs were rough beginnings; only Uncle Sun Tong gave a rough settling of court ritual.
translations.idiomatic: Appraisal says: At the beginning of Han, all affairs were rough beginnings; only Uncle Sun Tong gave a rough settling of court ritual.

sentences.zh: 若乃正朔服色郊望之事，數世猶未章焉。

translations.literal: Literal: As for calendrical beginning, dress color, suburban and distant sacrifices, even after several generations they were still not clear.
translations.idiomatic: As for calendrical beginning, dress color, suburban and distant sacrifices, even after several generations they were still not clear.

sentences.zh: 至於孝文，始以夏郊，而張倉據水德，公孫臣、賈誼更以為土德，卒不能明。

translations.literal: Literal: By Emperor Wen, summer suburban rite first began; yet Zhang Cang held to Water virtue, while Gongsun Chen and Jia Yi alternately argued for Earth virtue, and in the end it could not be clarified.
translations.idiomatic: By Emperor Wen, summer suburban rite first began; yet Zhang Cang held to Water virtue, while Gongsun Chen and Jia Yi alternately argued for Earth virtue, and in the end it could not be clarified.

sentences.zh: 孝武之世，文章為盛，太初改制，而兒寬、司馬遷等猶從臣、誼之言，服色數度，遂順黃德。

translations.literal: Literal: In Emperor Wu's age, literary culture flourished, and Taichu reforms changed institutions; yet Er Kuan and Sima Qian still followed Chen and Yi's position on dress colors and numerical degrees, eventually following Yellow virtue.
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Wu's age, literary culture flourished, and Taichu reforms changed institutions; yet Er Kuan and Sima Qian still followed Chen and Yi's position on dress colors and numerical degrees, eventually following Yellow virtue.

sentences.zh: 彼以五德之傳從所不勝，秦在水德，故謂漢據土而克之。

translations.literal: Literal: They took transmission of five virtues by conquering sequence: Qin in Water virtue, so Han was said to hold Earth and overcome it.
translations.idiomatic: They took transmission of five virtues by conquering sequence: Qin in Water virtue, so Han was said to hold Earth and overcome it.

sentences.zh: 劉向父子以為帝出於震，故包羲氏始受木德，其後以母傳子，終而復始，自神農、黃帝下歷唐虞三代而漢得火焉。

translations.literal: Literal: Liu Xiang and his son held that the Thearch emerges from Zhen; thus Fuxi first received Wood virtue, and then by mother-to-son transmission cycling to new beginning, from Shennong and Yellow Thearch through Tang, Yu, and three dynasties, Han obtained Fire virtue.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang and his son held that the Thearch emerges from Zhen; thus Fuxi first received Wood virtue, and then by mother-to-son transmission cycling to new beginning, from Shennong and Yellow Thearch through Tang, Yu, and three dynasties, Han obtained Fire virtue.

sentences.zh: 故高祖始起，神母夜號，著赤帝之符，旗章遂赤，自得天統矣。

translations.literal: Literal: Thus when Gaozu first arose, a spirit-mother cried at night and manifested the Red Thearch tally; banners and emblems accordingly became red, and Heaven's succession was naturally obtained.
translations.idiomatic: Thus when Gaozu first arose, a spirit-mother cried at night and manifested the Red Thearch tally; banners and emblems accordingly became red, and Heaven's succession was naturally obtained.

sentences.zh: 昔共工氏以水德間於木火，與秦同運，非其次序，故皆不永。

translations.literal: Literal: Formerly Gonggong lineage with Water virtue inserted between Wood and Fire, sharing Qin's cycle but not proper sequence, so neither endured long.
translations.idiomatic: Formerly Gonggong lineage with Water virtue inserted between Wood and Fire, sharing Qin's cycle but not proper sequence, so neither endured long.

sentences.zh: 由是言之，祖宗之制蓋有自然之應，順時宜矣。

translations.literal: Literal: From this we may say ancestral institutions likely had natural responses and were fitting to their times.
translations.idiomatic: From this we may say ancestral institutions likely had natural responses and were fitting to their times.

sentences.zh: 究觀方士祠官之變，谷永之言，不亦正乎！

translations.literal: Literal: Looking to the end at changes of fangshi and sacrificial officials, were Gu Yong's words not correct?
translations.idiomatic: Looking to the end at changes of fangshi and sacrificial officials, were Gu Yong's words not correct?

sentences.zh: 不亦正乎！

translations.literal: Literal: Were they not correct?
translations.idiomatic: Were they not correct?
